title: Restore Django Gemini backup into benchmark staged cache
description: Operational recovery task: restore the standalone Django Gemini backup DB into the live benchmark cache root as a staged interrupted build so the notebook's plain gloggur index resumes paid work instead of creating a fresh empty build.

title: Discover orphaned staged builds before cleanup
description:
Current shipped scope is more specific than the original recovery note. Index startup, `status --json`, and `search --json` now discover interrupted staged builds before any destructive cleanup path is taken. Orphaned manifest-backed staged builds are surfaced even when the root `.build-state.json` is missing or points at the wrong build, compatible candidates are ranked by refreshed staged-cache progress counts, and manifestless or incompatible staged directories are surfaced as blocked interrupted work instead of being silently deleted.

The shipped fail-closed behavior is that a fresh build does not start when interrupted staged work exists but none of it is safely resumable. In that case `index --json` returns `resume_blocked` plus candidate metadata and remediation, and the operator must either resume/adopt compatible work or explicitly pass `--discard-interrupted-builds` to throw staged work away. This issue records that discovery-before-cleanup contract only; it does not resolve the separate resume-identity mismatch still tracked in `bd-7jd`.
design:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_discover_resume_candidates(...)` is the core of the shipped fix. It reads both `list_staged_build_ids()` and `list_staged_build_resume_manifests()`, builds candidate payloads from every normalized manifest, and falls back to manifestless staged directories as explicit blocked candidates with `resume_manifest_missing` instead of treating them as cleanup junk.
- Candidate ranking is deterministic through `_resume_candidate_sort_key(...)` and refreshed staged-cache stats from `_refresh_resume_candidate_counts(...)` / `CacheManager.get_staged_build_index_stats(...)`, so resume selection prefers the most complete compatible interrupted build rather than only trusting the root build-state hint.
- `src/gloggur/indexer/cache.py:list_staged_build_resume_manifests()` gives startup deterministic manifest enumeration in build-id order; `cleanup_staged_builds()` remains destructive, so the important contract change is that CLI discovery runs before cleanup and cleanup requires either explicit discard or later normal publication/teardown paths.
- The `index` command path in `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` now performs resume discovery while the cache write lock is held, then chooses among four explicit outcomes: adopt a legacy interrupted build, auto-resume a compatible manifest-backed build, fail closed with `resume_blocked`, or start fresh only when no interrupted work blocks it (or when `--discard-interrupted-builds` is passed).
- Shared search/status health surfaces reuse the same discovery output through `_build_search_health_snapshot(...)`, `_build_search_not_ready_payload(...)`, and `_build_status_payload(...)`, which is why `status --json` and `search --json` now expose `interrupted_resume_available`, `resume_candidate`, `resume_blocked`, `resume_block_reason_codes`, and `resume_block_candidates`.
- This issue intentionally stops at discovery/blocking semantics. A candidate can still be discovered yet later rejected by compatibility/runtime checks, and the separate target-path-vs-cwd fingerprint mismatch remains tracked in `bd-7jd`.
acceptance_criteria:
- A manifest-backed interrupted staged build is discoverable and resumable even when persisted root build-state metadata is missing.
- A stale or wrong persisted build-state hint does not hide the real compatible interrupted build.
- When multiple compatible staged builds exist, resume selection is based on refreshed staged progress counts and deterministic ordering rather than arbitrary directory order.
- Manifestless or incompatible staged builds are surfaced as blocked interrupted work with reason codes and candidate metadata instead of being silently deleted at startup.
- `index --json` fails non-zero with `resume_blocked` when interrupted staged work exists but none is safely resumable, and a fresh build requires explicit operator intent via `--discard-interrupted-builds` or `--adopt-interrupted-build`.
- `status --json` and `search --json` expose the same interrupted-work discovery metadata and remediation guidance.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_discover_resume_candidates(...)` now enumerates manifests plus bare staged directories, synthesizes `resume_manifest_missing`, and returns `resume_block_reason_codes` / `resume_block_candidates` when interrupted work exists but is not resumable.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` index startup performs discovery before any explicit discard cleanup path, and `_build_index_resume_blocked_payload(...)` emits a stable `resume_blocked` failure contract.
- `src/gloggur/indexer/cache.py:list_staged_build_resume_manifests()` and `list_staged_build_ids()` provide deterministic staged-build enumeration.
- `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` now documents the shipped contract: compatible interrupted staged builds are discovered before cleanup, and status/search surface interrupted-resume metadata.
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `./.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_resume_recovery.py::test_discover_resume_candidates_finds_orphaned_manifest_without_persisted_build_state tests/unit/test_resume_recovery.py::test_discover_resume_candidates_ignores_missing_build_state_hint_and_selects_orphaned_build tests/unit/test_resume_recovery.py::test_discover_resume_candidates_ranks_using_refreshed_stage_counts tests/unit/test_resume_recovery.py::test_discover_resume_candidates_blocks_manifestless_or_incompatible_staged_work tests/unit/test_resume_recovery.py::test_status_payload_reports_orphaned_resume_candidate_without_persisted_build_state tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_build_search_not_ready_payload_uses_resume_blocked_guidance tests/integration/test_resume_recovery_integration.py::test_index_auto_resumes_orphaned_staged_build_without_persisted_build_state tests/integration/test_resume_recovery_integration.py::test_index_blocks_manifestless_staged_build_until_discard_is_explicit`
  - result: `8 passed, 3 warnings in 3.20s`
- Important scope boundary:
  - this shipped issue hardened discovery and fail-closed cleanup behavior around orphaned staged builds
  - the separate resume-identity mismatch where compatibility can still fail because of target-path vs caller-cwd fingerprinting remains tracked by `bd-7jd`

title: Bind interrupted-build resume identity to index target path instead of cwd
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_write_staged_build_resume_manifest(...)`
  - already writes `workspace_path_hash` from `_current_workspace_path_hash(index_target_path)` and also stores `index_target_path` explicitly in the staged resume manifest.
  - this is the stronger identity source that the rest of the resume contract should align with.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_build_resume_candidate_from_manifest(...)`
  - already uses `normalized_index_target_path` and/or the manifest's stored `index_target_path` to decide whether a `workspace_path_hash` mismatch should be ignored for legacy cwd-bound manifests.
  - current behavior intentionally trusts the stored target path when it matches, so manifest-based interrupted-build recovery is partially fixed already.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_build_resume_contract(...)`
  - still computes `workspace_path_hash = _hash_content(os.path.abspath(os.getcwd()))` and uses that hash for both `expected_resume_fingerprint` and `cached_resume_fingerprint`.
  - because both values are derived from the current caller cwd, `resume_fingerprint_match` can remain `true` even when the persisted last-success marker came from a different cwd.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_persist_last_success_resume_state(...)`
  - still repairs/persists `last_success_resume_fingerprint` using `os.getcwd()` rather than the indexed target path that produced the cache contents.
  - this is why the plain console entrypoint can record a different last-success identity for the same cache depending on launch location.
- launcher boundary:
  - `scripts/gloggur` defaults to `cd "$REPO_ROOT"` unless `GLOGGUR_RUN_FROM_CALLER_CWD=1` is set;
  - `.venv/bin/gloggur` imports `gloggur.cli.main` directly and keeps the caller cwd.
  - regression coverage for this issue should therefore exercise the plain console entrypoint or explicitly force caller-cwd preservation so wrapper behavior does not hide the bug.
- test touchpoints:
  - `tests/unit/test_resume_recovery.py` and `tests/integration/test_resume_recovery_integration.py` already cover legacy cwd-bound manifest tolerance;
  - `tests/integration/test_resume_contract_integration.py` covers stable same-cwd fingerprints across unchanged reindex;
  - a remaining gap is explicit regression coverage for cross-cwd last-success fingerprint drift when target path and cache contents are unchanged.

Recommended implementation direction:
- thread canonical target identity into `_build_resume_contract(...)` and `_persist_last_success_resume_state(...)` instead of deriving it from ambient cwd;
- keep backward compatibility for older last-success fingerprints only where necessary, similar to the manifest-tolerance path;
- avoid relying on `scripts/gloggur` default cwd normalization as the de facto fix, because other entrypoints still surface the mismatch.
acceptance_criteria:
- Resume identity for staged manifests, status/search metadata, and persisted last-success markers is derived from the indexed target path (or an equivalent canonical target identity), not ambient caller cwd.
- Running `status --json` or `search --json` from a different cwd against the same unchanged cache does not flip `last_success_resume_fingerprint_match` solely because the command was launched elsewhere.
- Legacy staged manifests whose `workspace_path_hash` was captured from caller cwd remain resumable when their stored `index_target_path` matches the requested target.
- The bootstrap wrapper and the plain console entrypoint expose the same resume-identity semantics; wrapper cwd normalization must not be required to obtain correct resume metadata.
- Regression coverage exists for both already-supported legacy-manifest tolerance and the remaining cross-cwd last-success fingerprint case.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_write_staged_build_resume_manifest(...)` already persists `workspace_path_hash` from `index_target_path` and records `index_target_path` in the manifest.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_build_resume_candidate_from_manifest(...)` already prefers `normalized_index_target_path` / manifest `index_target_path` when deciding whether to ignore `workspace_path_hash` mismatch for legacy manifests.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_build_resume_contract(...)` still derives `workspace_path_hash` from `os.getcwd()`.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_persist_last_success_resume_state(...)` still derives the last-success fingerprint from `os.getcwd()`.
- Launcher split confirmed from current files:
  - `scripts/gloggur` does `cd "$REPO_ROOT"` unless `GLOGGUR_RUN_FROM_CALLER_CWD=1` is set.
  - `.venv/bin/gloggur` is a plain console script that imports `gloggur.cli.main` directly and preserves caller cwd.
- Direct probe with `.venv/bin/gloggur` on 2026-03-26:
  - indexed a temporary repo into a temporary cache from one cwd;
  - then ran `status --json` from a different cwd against the same cache;
  - result stayed `needs_reindex = false` and `resume_decision = "resume_ok"`, but `last_success_resume_fingerprint_match = false` solely because caller cwd changed.
  - in that same probe, `resume_fingerprint_match` remained `true` because both `expected_resume_fingerprint` and `cached_resume_fingerprint` were recomputed from the new caller cwd, which underlines that the misleading signal is specifically in the persisted last-success marker.
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `./.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_resume_recovery.py::test_resume_candidate_payload_tolerates_legacy_cwd_bound_manifest_when_target_matches tests/integration/test_resume_recovery_integration.py::test_index_auto_resumes_legacy_cwd_bound_manifest_when_target_path_matches tests/integration/test_resume_contract_integration.py::test_resume_fingerprint_stable_across_unchanged_reindex`
  - result: `3 passed, 3 warnings in 6.42s`
- Scope clarification:
  - this issue is no longer a raw “resume always rejects wrong-cwd manifests” bug;
  - manifest-based interrupted-build recovery already has target-path-aware tolerance;
  - the remaining work is to unify the broader resume fingerprint / last-success contract with that same target-path identity.

title: Add support for YAML/YML parsing and structural symbol extraction
description:
Current code does not support YAML/YML as indexed source today, even though YAML is already part of the operator surface for `.gloggur.yaml` config files and the query layer already treats `.yaml` and `.yml` as plausible file-path targets. `.yaml` and `.yml` are absent from the default `supported_extensions` list and from `ParserRegistry`'s builtin extension map, so `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path(...)` returns `None` for both and `is_indexable_source_path(...)` rejects YAML files under the default config.

This issue should implement first-class YAML support as a parser and contract feature, not as a config-only extension toggle. Adding `.yaml` and `.yml` to `supported_extensions` alone currently fails extension-policy validation because there is no parser mapping. Adding `parser_extension_map` entries for `yaml` makes validation pass, but runtime still cannot parse YAML because `ParserRegistry` has no builtin `yaml` adapter and `TreeSitterParser("yaml")` currently raises `ValueError: Unsupported language: yaml`. The installed `tree_sitter_language_pack` already exposes a raw YAML parser, so the real work is repo integration and structural symbol modeling rather than missing parser binaries.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- Raw tree-sitter grammar support for YAML is already available in the dependency set, but no repo-level YAML adapter or published support contract exists.
- The current `TreeSitterParser` abstraction is oriented around function/class/interface extraction, which is not a clean direct fit for YAML mapping, sequence, anchor, alias, and merge semantics.
- Existing line/byte/span models already fit plain-text YAML files, so the hard part is choosing deterministic structural symbol semantics rather than file ingestion.
- Current symbol-index and graph-reference extraction paths are code-oriented, so YAML support needs an explicit decision about whether anchors, aliases, merge keys, and key paths become symbols, signals, attributes, or dedicated edges instead of forcing them into import/call/reference heuristics.
- There is no YAML entry in the published language support contract or parser capability corpus today.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/config.py`, `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py`, and `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py`: add `.yaml` and `.yml` to the default support contract only alongside a real parser adapter and parser-capability coverage. Do not ship YAML as a config-only extension override that passes validation but fails at runtime.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py` versus a dedicated YAML adapter: given the current `LanguageSpec` model only understands function/class/interface node families, the safer implementation path is likely a dedicated YAML parser adapter that walks tree-sitter YAML nodes such as `block_mapping`, `block_mapping_pair`, `block_sequence`, `anchor`, and `alias`, then emits structural symbols and signals directly. This is an inference from the current parser design, not an already-implemented contract.
- `src/gloggur/models.py` and `src/gloggur/byte_spans.py`: reuse the existing file/span contracts; YAML fits the current one-file contiguous-text model.
- `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py`: define whether YAML contributes reference vocabulary at all and, if so, through explicit key-path/anchor semantics rather than reusing the current ASCII code-identifier tokenizer unchanged.
- `src/gloggur/graph/extractor.py`: decide whether phase 1 emits no graph edges, only deterministic anchor/alias/merge edges, or simple containment-style edges. The current import/call/reference regexes are not a YAML graph model.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` plus parser-support tests: publish YAML in support diagnostics and capability output only when the parser path and fixture corpus exist.

Recommended minimum scope for this feature:
- deterministic structural extraction for representative mapping/sequence YAML documents;
- explicit treatment of anchors, aliases, and merge keys;
- explicit non-goal for arbitrary scalar leaf values becoming standalone symbols unless a concrete retrieval need justifies that expansion.
acceptance_criteria:
- `.yaml` and `.yml` are part of the default supported extension contract and resolve to a real parser adapter without requiring operator-specific parser overrides.
- The language support contract and `gloggur parsers check --json` include YAML with explicit construct tiers and built-in fixture coverage.
- YAML parsing yields deterministic structural symbols for representative mapping/sequence documents instead of treating YAML as parser-unavailable text.
- Anchor, alias, and merge-key behavior is either implemented deterministically or published as explicit known gaps with parser-check coverage and fail-loud behavior where needed.
- Status/inspect/index diagnostics, tests, and docs reflect the final YAML support scope for both `.yaml` and `.yml`.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- Repo-wide search for YAML support in parser code only found YAML used for config/workflow loading and path heuristics, not as an indexed source language.
- `src/gloggur/config.py` default `supported_extensions` omit both `.yaml` and `.yml`.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py` has no `.yaml` or `.yml` builtin extension map entries and no builtin `yaml` adapter id.
- Direct support probes:
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("sample.yaml") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("sample.yml") -> None`
  - `is_indexable_source_path("sample.yaml", ...) -> False`
  - `is_indexable_source_path("sample.yml", ...) -> False`
  - adding `.yaml` and `.yml` to `supported_extensions` without parser overrides fails `_validate_extension_policy(...)` with `StorageIOError: supported_extensions missing parser mappings: .yaml, .yml`
  - adding `parser_extension_map={'.yaml': 'yaml', '.yml': 'yaml'}` makes extension-policy validation pass, but `ParserRegistry(extension_map=...).get_parser_for_path("sample.yaml")` then fails with `AdapterResolutionError: Unknown adapter 'yaml' for group 'gloggur.parsers'`
- Raw grammar availability is already present:
  - `tree_sitter_language_pack.get_parser("yaml")` returned a parser
  - `TreeSitterParser("yaml")` currently raises `ValueError: Unsupported language: yaml` because `_LANGUAGE_SPECS` excludes YAML
- Raw sample YAML grammar shapes from the installed parser included named nodes such as `stream`, `document`, `block_mapping`, `block_mapping_pair`, `plain_scalar`, `block_sequence`, `anchor`, and `alias`.
- Current model compatibility:
  - `src/gloggur/byte_spans.py:LineByteSpanIndex` and the `Symbol`/`SymbolChunk` span contracts already fit plain-text YAML files.
  - `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` and `src/gloggur/graph/extractor.py` still use code-oriented reference tokenization and import/call semantics, so YAML alias/merge relationships need an explicit model rather than incidental regex reuse.
- Query-layer clue:
  - `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_FIND_IMPLICIT_PATHLIKE_EXTENSIONS` already includes `.yaml` and `.yml`, so command parsing already treats them as plausible file-path targets even though structural indexing does not support them today.
- Contract-gap clue:
  - `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py` and `tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py` contain no YAML language contract tiers or parser-capability cases today.

title: Add support for Makefile parsing and target symbol modeling
description:
Current code does not support Makefile-family files as indexed source today, and the real blocker is file identification as much as parser wiring. The repo's current support contract is built around normalized dot-prefixed extension tokens, while canonical Makefiles are often basename-driven (`Makefile`, `GNUmakefile`) rather than extension-driven. Under the default config, `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path(...)` returns `None` for `Makefile`, `GNUmakefile`, `docs/Makefile`, and `build.mk`, and `is_indexable_source_path(...)` rejects all of them.

This issue should therefore be treated as a detection + parser + symbol-model feature, not merely “add one more tree-sitter language”. The installed `tree_sitter_language_pack` already exposes a raw `make` parser, but the repo cannot use it today because there is no builtin `make` adapter and `TreeSitterParser("make")` currently raises `ValueError: Unsupported language: make`. Configuration overrides can be made to validate only by passing normalized pseudo-extensions such as `.makefile`, `.gnumakefile`, and `.mk`, which means the current extension-policy contract does not model Makefile basenames explicitly even though runtime matching is based on `endswith(...)`.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- Makefile-family files are outside the default support contract and parser registry today.
- Raw tree-sitter grammar support for `make` is already present in the dependency set, but there is no repo-level parser adapter or parser-capability corpus for it.
- The current `TreeSitterParser` abstraction is oriented around function/class/interface extraction and is not a clean direct fit for make rules, pattern rules, variable assignments, include directives, and recipe bodies.
- Query/retrieval already show Makefiles matter in practice: router path priors classify `docs/Makefile` as a docs build config authority path, and exact-backend/router tests already use `docs/Makefile` examples.
- Existing file/span/chunk models already fit plain-text Makefiles, so the hard part is deterministic modeling of targets, prerequisites, variables, includes, and recipe semantics rather than raw file ingestion.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/config.py`, `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`, `src/gloggur/path_filters.py`, and `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py`: define how Makefile-family detection should work in a contract that currently normalizes every supported file token into dot-prefixed lowercase values. The current behavior turns `Makefile` into `.makefile` and `GNUmakefile` into `.gnumakefile` during validation, which is workable only as an implicit compatibility trick. The feature should make basename support explicit or consciously standardize the pseudo-extension approach and verify it end-to-end.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py` versus a dedicated Make adapter: given the current `LanguageSpec` model only understands callable/type declaration families, a dedicated Make parser adapter is likely cleaner than forcing make rules into function/class semantics. This is an inference from the current parser design, not an already-implemented contract.
- Raw `make` grammar probes show named nodes such as `include_directive`, `variable_assignment`, `rule`, `targets`, `prerequisites`, `recipe`, `variable_reference`, and `automatic_variable`; those should drive the first-pass symbol model.
- `src/gloggur/models.py`, `src/gloggur/byte_spans.py`, and retrieval span models can be reused directly because Makefiles are plain text with contiguous line/byte spans.
- `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` and `src/gloggur/graph/extractor.py` need an explicit Makefile model instead of the current code-oriented token/import/call heuristics. Reasonable phase-1 graph candidates are include edges and target->prerequisite edges; recipe shell bodies should probably remain opaque text in phase 1 rather than being interpreted as shell graph edges.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py` and parser-check tests need a published `make` contract with built-in fixture cases before support is advertised.

Recommended minimum scope for this feature:
- detect `Makefile`, `GNUmakefile`, and `.mk` files deterministically;
- extract structural symbols for variable assignments and target rules;
- explicitly scope how include directives, pattern rules, phony/special targets, and recipe bodies are handled;
- document any GNU-make-only constructs that remain unsupported.
acceptance_criteria:
- The default support contract covers the intended Makefile family explicitly, including `Makefile`, `GNUmakefile`, and any supported extension-based variants such as `.mk`.
- Parser resolution succeeds for supported Makefile-family paths without requiring operator-specific adapter overrides.
- The language support contract and `gloggur parsers check --json` include `make` with explicit construct tiers and built-in fixture coverage.
- Parsing yields deterministic structural symbols for representative makefiles, including variable assignments and rule targets; pattern rules, include directives, and special targets are either implemented deterministically or published as explicit known gaps.
- Index/inspect/status diagnostics, tests, and docs reflect the final Makefile-family detection rules and support scope.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- Repo-wide search found no Makefile parser implementation or language-support docs. The main existing Makefile-aware logic is in retrieval heuristics: `src/gloggur/search/router/path_priors.py` treats `makefile` under `docs/` as a docs build config authority path, and tests such as `tests/unit/test_search_router.py` and `tests/unit/test_search_exact_backend.py` already use `docs/Makefile` examples.
- `src/gloggur/config.py` default `supported_extensions` omit `.mk`, and there is no default support token for `Makefile` or `GNUmakefile`.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py` has no builtin `make` adapter id and no Makefile-family path mapping.
- Direct support probes:
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("Makefile") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("GNUmakefile") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("docs/Makefile") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("build.mk") -> None`
  - `is_indexable_source_path(...)` returned `False` for all of the above paths under the default config
  - adding `Makefile` and `GNUmakefile` to `supported_extensions` without parser overrides fails `_validate_extension_policy(...)` with `StorageIOError: supported_extensions missing parser mappings: .gnumakefile, .makefile`
  - adding parser overrides for `Makefile` / `GNUmakefile` / `.mk` validates only after normalization to `.makefile`, `.gnumakefile`, and `.mk`, but runtime still fails because `ParserRegistry(extension_map=...).get_parser_for_path("Makefile")` raises `AdapterResolutionError: Unknown adapter 'make' for group 'gloggur.parsers'`
- Raw grammar availability is already present:
  - `tree_sitter_language_pack.get_parser("make")` returned a parser
  - `tree_sitter_language_pack.get_parser("makefile")` and `get_parser("gnumake")` both failed lookup
  - `TreeSitterParser("make")` currently raises `ValueError: Unsupported language: make` because `_LANGUAGE_SPECS` excludes it
- Raw sample `make` grammar shapes from the installed parser included named nodes such as `makefile`, `include_directive`, `variable_assignment`, `rule`, `targets`, `prerequisites`, `recipe`, `variable_reference`, and `automatic_variable`.
- Published-contract gap:
  - `build_language_support_contract()` does not include `make` in `enabled_languages`, does not include `.mk` or any Makefile token in `supported_extensions`, and `run_parser_capability_check(...)` currently emits no `make` cases.
- Current model constraints:
  - `src/gloggur/models.py` and `src/gloggur/byte_spans.py` already fit plain-text Makefiles.
  - `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` and `src/gloggur/graph/extractor.py` still assume code-like identifier/import/call vocabularies, so Make target/prerequisite/include relationships need an explicit model rather than incidental reuse of those regexes.

title: Add support for SQL parsing and symbol extraction
description:
Current code does not support SQL files as indexed source today, and the gap is broader than “one missing parser registration”. `.sql` is absent from the default `supported_extensions` list and from `ParserRegistry`'s builtin extension map, so `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path(...)` returns `None` for `.sql` files and `is_indexable_source_path(...)` rejects them under the default config. At the same time, the query layer already treats `.sql` as a plausible path-like suffix for `find`, which means users can naturally ask about SQL files even though the indexer cannot parse or structurally index them.

This issue should therefore be treated as a file-detection + parser + symbol-model feature. The installed `tree_sitter_language_pack` already exposes a raw `sql` parser, but the repo cannot use it today because there is no builtin `sql` adapter and `TreeSitterParser("sql")` currently raises `ValueError: Unsupported language: sql`. Adding `.sql` to `supported_extensions` alone fails extension-policy validation because there is no parser mapping. Adding `parser_extension_map={'.sql': 'sql'}` makes validation pass, but runtime still fails because `ParserRegistry` has no builtin `sql` adapter.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- `.sql` files are outside the default support contract, parser registry, and parser capability corpus today.
- Raw tree-sitter grammar support for generic `sql` is already present in the dependency set, but there is no repo-level parser adapter or published contract for it.
- The current `TreeSitterParser` abstraction is oriented around function/class/interface extraction and is not a clean direct fit for tables, views, indexes, schemas, CTEs, and query statements.
- Query routing does not currently treat `.sql` as source code: `is_source_code_path("schema.sql")` is `False` and the code-domain path prior falls back to the generic `0.66` score rather than the `1.0` source-code prior.
- Existing line/byte/span models already fit plain-text SQL files, so the main work is deciding deterministic symbol semantics and explicit dialect boundaries rather than file ingestion.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/config.py`, `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py`, `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py`, and `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`: add `.sql` to the default support contract only alongside a real parser adapter and parser-capability coverage. Do not ship SQL as a config-only extension override that validates but still fails at runtime.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py` versus a dedicated SQL adapter: given the current `LanguageSpec` model only understands callable/type declaration families, a dedicated SQL parser adapter is likely cleaner than forcing table/view/index/query constructs into function/class semantics. This is an inference from the current parser design, not an already-implemented contract.
- Raw `sql` grammar probes show named nodes such as `create_table`, `column_definition`, `create_index`, `create_view`, `create_query`, `select`, `relation`, and `join`; those should drive the first-pass structural symbol model and parser-check fixtures.
- `src/gloggur/models.py` and `src/gloggur/byte_spans.py` can be reused directly because SQL files are plain text with contiguous line/byte spans.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/path_priors.py` should be revisited once SQL is supported. Today `.sql` is path-like but not source-like; a first-class SQL feature should decide whether `.sql` belongs in source-code path priors for code-domain retrieval.
- `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` and `src/gloggur/graph/extractor.py` need an explicit SQL model instead of the current code-token/import/call heuristics. Reasonable phase-1 graph candidates are object-definition containment and relation references inside queries; database execution semantics should remain out of scope.

Recommended minimum scope for this feature:
- deterministic extraction for common DDL objects such as tables, views, and indexes;
- explicit decision on whether functions/procedures/triggers are in phase 1 or deferred;
- explicit contract for dialect handling, especially where generic `sql` grammar support ends and dialect-specific syntax becomes a known gap.
acceptance_criteria:
- `.sql` is part of the default supported extension contract and resolves to a real parser adapter without requiring operator-specific parser overrides.
- The language support contract and `gloggur parsers check --json` include `sql` with explicit construct tiers and built-in fixture coverage.
- Parsing yields deterministic structural symbols for representative SQL files, at minimum covering common DDL objects such as tables, views, and indexes.
- Dialect-specific syntax is either supported intentionally or published as explicit known gaps with parser-check coverage and fail-loud behavior where needed.
- Index/inspect/status diagnostics, path-prior behavior, tests, and docs reflect the final SQL support scope.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- Repo-wide search found no SQL parser implementation or language-support docs. Existing `sql` hits are almost entirely about SQLite storage internals rather than `.sql` source-file support.
- `src/gloggur/config.py` default `supported_extensions` omit `.sql`.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py` has no `.sql` builtin extension map entry and no builtin `sql` adapter id.
- Direct support probes:
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("schema.sql") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("db/migrations/001_init.sql") -> None`
  - `is_indexable_source_path("schema.sql", ...) -> False`
  - `is_indexable_source_path("db/migrations/001_init.sql", ...) -> False`
  - adding `.sql` to `supported_extensions` without parser overrides fails `_validate_extension_policy(...)` with `StorageIOError: supported_extensions missing parser mappings: .sql`
  - adding `parser_extension_map={'.sql': 'sql'}` makes extension-policy validation pass, but `ParserRegistry(extension_map=...).get_parser_for_path("schema.sql")` then fails with `AdapterResolutionError: Unknown adapter 'sql' for group 'gloggur.parsers'`
- Raw grammar availability is already present:
  - `tree_sitter_language_pack.get_parser("sql")` returned a parser
  - `tree_sitter_language_pack.get_parser("postgres")` and `get_parser("sqlite")` both failed lookup in this installed dependency set
  - `TreeSitterParser("sql")` currently raises `ValueError: Unsupported language: sql` because `_LANGUAGE_SPECS` excludes SQL
- Raw sample `sql` grammar shapes from the installed parser included named nodes such as `create_table`, `column_definition`, `create_index`, `create_view`, `create_query`, `select`, `relation`, and `join`.
- Published-contract gap:
  - `build_language_support_contract()` does not include `sql` in `enabled_languages`, does not include `.sql` in `supported_extensions`, and `run_parser_capability_check(...)` currently emits no `sql` cases.
- Retrieval/path-prior gap:
  - `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_FIND_IMPLICIT_PATHLIKE_EXTENSIONS` already includes `.sql`, so command parsing treats SQL paths as plausible file targets.
  - `src/gloggur/search/router/path_priors.py` does not treat `.sql` as source code today: `is_source_code_path("schema.sql") -> False` and `path_domain_score("schema.sql", query_domain="code") -> 0.66`.
- Current model constraints:
  - `src/gloggur/models.py` and `src/gloggur/byte_spans.py` already fit plain-text SQL files.
  - `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` and `src/gloggur/graph/extractor.py` still assume code-like token/import/call vocabularies, so SQL object/reference relationships need an explicit model rather than incidental reuse of those regexes.

title: Add support for shell script family parsing (bash/zsh/sh/dash/ksh)
description:
Current code does not support shell-family scripts as indexed source today, despite the repo itself containing bash entrypoints under `scripts/` and the query layer already treating some shell suffixes as plausible path targets. `.sh`, `.bash`, `.zsh`, `.ksh`, and `.dash` are absent from the default `supported_extensions` list and from `ParserRegistry`'s builtin extension map, so `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path(...)` returns `None` and `is_indexable_source_path(...)` rejects them under the default config.

This is broader than a one-line extension-map addition. File eligibility and parser selection are currently suffix-only, so common extensionless shebang scripts such as `bin/deploy` are also unsupported even if they start with `#!/usr/bin/env bash`. Adding shell extensions to `supported_extensions` alone currently fails extension-policy validation because there is no parser mapping. Adding a config override that maps shell extensions to `bash` makes validation pass, but runtime still fails because `ParserRegistry` has no builtin `bash` adapter and `TreeSitterParser("bash")` is unsupported.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- The installed `tree_sitter_language_pack` already exposes a raw `bash` parser, but not separate `sh`/`zsh`/`ksh`/`dash` parsers in this dependency set.
- The current `TreeSitterParser` abstraction is oriented around function/class/interface extraction, which is not a clean fit for shell constructs such as sourced files, simple commands, pipelines, case arms, subshells, and variable assignments.
- Query/path heuristics are inconsistent with the issue title today: `_FIND_IMPLICIT_PATHLIKE_EXTENSIONS` recognizes `.bash`, `.sh`, and `.zsh`, but not `.ksh` or `.dash`, while code-domain path priors treat none of them as source code.
- Existing line/byte/span models already fit plain-text shell scripts, so the hard part is choosing symbol/edge semantics and an explicit compatibility contract for bash-vs-other-shell behavior rather than file ingestion itself.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/config.py`, `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py`, `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py`, and `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`: add shell-family support to the default contract only alongside a real parser adapter and parser-capability coverage. Do not ship shell files as config-only extension overrides that validate but still fail at runtime.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py` versus a dedicated shell adapter: because the current `LanguageSpec` model only understands callable/type declaration families, a dedicated `bash`/shell adapter is likely cleaner than forcing shell nodes into function/class/interface buckets. This is an inference from the current parser design, not an already-implemented contract.
- Raw `bash` grammar probes already expose named nodes such as `function_definition`, `command`, `command_name`, `declaration_command`, `variable_assignment`, and `for_statement`; those should drive the first-pass fixture corpus and structural extraction model.
- `src/gloggur/path_filters.py` and `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py`: decide whether phase 1 remains extension-based (`.sh`, `.bash`, possibly `.zsh`) or whether extensionless shebang scripts become part of the contract. Today both indexing and parser resolution are pure suffix checks.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/path_priors.py`: once shell support exists, decide whether shell files should be treated as code-domain source paths instead of generic `0.66` fallback candidates.
- `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` and `src/gloggur/graph/extractor.py`: define shell-specific semantics explicitly instead of reusing current code-language identifier/import/call heuristics unchanged. Likely phase-1 candidates are function definitions plus deterministic `source`/`.` file references; environment variables, aliases, and generic command invocations need an explicit noise policy.

Recommended minimum scope for the first implementation:
- treat `bash` as the concrete parser baseline because that is the raw grammar actually available today;
- support `.sh` and `.bash` first, then either map `.zsh`/`.ksh`/`.dash` to that baseline with published compatibility limits or keep them explicit known gaps;
- make extensionless shebang handling an explicit yes/no decision rather than an accidental omission.
acceptance_criteria:
- The default support contract includes the shell file types intentionally supported in phase 1 and resolves them to a real builtin parser adapter without requiring operator-specific parser overrides.
- The language support contract and `gloggur parsers check --json` include shell/baseline cases with explicit construct tiers and fixture coverage.
- Parsing yields deterministic structural symbols for representative shell scripts, at minimum covering function definitions and any chosen sourced-file/reference semantics.
- Bash-vs-zsh/dash/ksh compatibility is either intentionally implemented or published as explicit known gaps, rather than implied by the issue title without tests.
- If extensionless shebang scripts remain unsupported, that limitation is explicit in docs/contract/tests; otherwise indexing and parser resolution support them deterministically.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- Repo-wide search found shell usage in bootstrap/test scripts and CLI pathlike heuristics, but no shell parser implementation or published shell support contract.
- `src/gloggur/config.py` default `supported_extensions` omit `.sh`, `.bash`, `.zsh`, `.ksh`, and `.dash`.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py` has no shell-family builtin extension map entries and no builtin `bash`/shell adapter id.
- Direct support probes:
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("script.sh") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("script.bash") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("script.zsh") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("script.ksh") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("script.dash") -> None`
  - `ParserRegistry().get_parser_for_path("bin/deploy") -> None`
  - `is_indexable_source_path(...) -> False` for all of the above under default config
  - adding shell extensions to `supported_extensions` without parser overrides fails `_validate_extension_policy(...)` with `StorageIOError: supported_extensions missing parser mappings: .bash, .dash, .ksh, .sh, .zsh`
  - adding `parser_extension_map` entries that map those suffixes to `bash` makes validation pass, but `ParserRegistry(extension_map=...).get_parser_for_path(...)` then fails with `AdapterResolutionError: Unknown adapter 'bash' for group 'gloggur.parsers'`
- Raw grammar availability is partial:
  - `tree_sitter_language_pack.get_parser("bash")` returned a parser
  - `get_parser("sh")`, `get_parser("shell")`, `get_parser("zsh")`, `get_parser("ksh")`, and `get_parser("dash")` all failed lookup in this installed dependency set
  - `TreeSitterParser("bash")` currently raises `ValueError: Unsupported language: bash` because `_LANGUAGE_SPECS` excludes it
- Raw sample `bash` grammar shapes from the installed parser included named nodes such as `program`, `function_definition`, `command`, `command_name`, `declaration_command`, `variable_assignment`, `for_statement`, and `do_group`.
- Query/routing gaps:
  - `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_FIND_IMPLICIT_PATHLIKE_EXTENSIONS` already includes `.bash`, `.sh`, and `.zsh`, but not `.ksh` or `.dash`
  - `src/gloggur/search/router/path_priors.py` does not treat shell files as source code today: `is_source_code_path("script.sh") -> False`, `is_source_code_path("script.bash") -> False`, `is_source_code_path("script.zsh") -> False`, and code-domain `path_domain_score(...)` falls back to `0.66`
- Current model constraints:
  - `src/gloggur/path_filters.py` and `ParserRegistry.get_parser_for_path(...)` are suffix-based, so extensionless shebang scripts are outside the current contract
  - `src/gloggur/models.py` and `src/gloggur/byte_spans.py` already fit plain-text shell files
  - `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` and `src/gloggur/graph/extractor.py` still assume ASCII code-identifier and language-specific import/call patterns, so shell reference semantics need an explicit model instead of incidental regex reuse.

title: Close remaining C/C++ parser gaps after bd-phx
description:
This issue was the focused C/C++ gap-closure pass discovered from `bd-phx`, and the shipped scope is more specific than the original one-line summary. Current code already treats these behaviors as part of the baseline C/C++ parser contract: C-family declarator handling distinguishes callback-returning function declarations from plain function-pointer variables, C++ owner resolution normalizes namespace-qualified and out-of-class method fqnames, template-specialized and operator methods keep stable names, and a narrow class of macro-generated methods/functions is recovered when the macro expansion matches strict placeholder patterns.

The issue is therefore best understood as parser-contract hardening rather than generic “more C++ support”. It tightened positive and negative extraction cases so the repo can publish C/C++ baseline behavior through `gloggur parsers check --json` and the support contract without overstating capabilities. In current repo state, the remaining explicit boundary is narrow: C has no published known-gap constructs, and C++ only leaves complex macro-generated forms outside strict placeholder patterns as a known gap.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- `c.callback_returning_function_pointer` and `c.function_pointer_variable_not_callable` both pass in parser capability checks, confirming deterministic callable/non-callable declarator classification.
- `cpp.class_and_qualified_methods`, `cpp.namespace_qualified_methods`, `cpp.template_and_operator_methods`, and `cpp.macro_generated_method_recovery` all pass and are published as baseline C++ constructs.
- Direct parser probes confirm `make_cb` extracts as a function, plain `fp` emits no callable symbol, `core.Box<int>.get` and `Vec.operator[]` survive fqname normalization, and `DECL_METHOD(Greeter, ping)` recovers `Greeter.ping` while a more complex placeholder macro form does not.
- The practical residual gap is no longer declarator or fqname instability; it is bounded macro complexity beyond the published strict recovery patterns.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py`
  - `_c_family_declarator_info(...)`, `_c_family_declarator_path(...)`, and `_c_family_qualified_parts(...)` are the core C-family declarator walkers. They decide whether a nested declarator is a callable declaration, extract the terminal symbol name, and preserve qualified owner parts for C++ method resolution.
  - `_cpp_namespace_container(...)`, `_cpp_has_qualified_method_declarator(...)`, and `_cpp_method_container_from_qualified_declarator(...)` are the container/fqname normalization path for namespace-qualified and out-of-class C++ methods.
  - `_collect_cpp_macro_patterns(...)`, `_cpp_macro_pattern_from_definition(...)`, `_cpp_macro_symbol_specs(...)`, and `_drop_cpp_macro_duplicates(...)` implement the intentionally narrow macro-recovery contract and prevent duplicate explicit/macro-generated method symbols.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py`
  - publishes the C baseline constructs `function_definition`, `function_declaration`, `struct_union_declaration`, `enum_declaration`, and `function_pointer_declarator`.
  - publishes the C++ baseline constructs `class_struct_declaration`, `class_body_method_definition`, `class_body_method_declaration`, `qualified_method_definition`, `namespace_qualified_container_fqname`, `template_operator_normalization`, and `macro_generated_recoverable_patterns`.
  - keeps `macro_generated_complex_forms` as the only explicit C++ known gap in this area.
  - defines the required parser-capability cases that lock the intended declarator/fqname/macro boundary.
- `tests/unit/test_parsers.py`
  - contains the parser regression tests for C callable declarators, function-pointer negatives, qualified C++ owners, namespace/template/operator naming, and strict macro recovery.
- `tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py`
  - verifies that the support contract publishes the same construct tiers and parser-check cases that the parser actually satisfies.
- `README.md` and `docs/QUICKSTART.md`
  - already advertise C/C++ support and point users at parser diagnostics when fidelity boundaries matter.

Recommended scope interpretation:
- treat `bd-ygl` as the precision-hardening slice that made the C/C++ support contract truthful and test-backed after `bd-phx`;
- do not reinterpret it as full support for arbitrary macro-generated C++ symbols or broader metaprogramming recovery.
acceptance_criteria:
- C-family declarator extraction deterministically classifies callback-returning function-pointer declarators as callable functions while leaving plain function-pointer variables out of the symbol table.
- C++ extraction preserves stable fqnames for class-body methods, namespace-qualified out-of-class methods, template-specialized methods, and operator methods.
- Strictly recoverable macro-generated C++ method/function patterns are extracted and deduplicated against explicit declarations.
- More complex macro-generated C++ placeholder forms remain explicit known-gap behavior instead of silent partial extraction.
- `gloggur parsers check --json`, the published language support contract, unit tests, and docs all reflect the same C/C++ scope boundary.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `scripts/gloggur parsers check --json` reports:
  - `ok: true`
  - `required_case_counts.passed: 24`
  - required passing C/C++ cases:
    - `c.functions_and_types`
    - `c.callback_returning_function_pointer`
    - `c.function_pointer_variable_not_callable`
    - `cpp.class_and_qualified_methods`
    - `cpp.namespace_qualified_methods`
    - `cpp.template_and_operator_methods`
    - `cpp.macro_generated_method_recovery`
- `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py` currently publishes:
  - C constructs as all-baseline with no C known gaps
  - C++ baseline constructs for qualified methods, namespace-qualified container fqnames, template/operator normalization, and recoverable macro patterns
  - one remaining C++ known gap: `macro-generated symbols outside strict placeholder patterns are not extracted`
- `tests/unit/test_parsers.py` currently locks the concrete parser behavior:
  - callback-returning function-pointer detection
  - skipping plain function-pointer variables
  - qualified C++ method owner recovery
  - namespace/template/operator fqname normalization
  - bounded macro-generated method recovery and deduplication
- Direct parser probes on 2026-03-26 confirmed:
  - `int (*make_cb(void))(int);` -> `function make_cb`
  - `int (*fp)(int);` -> no symbols
  - namespace/template/operator sample emits `core.Box`, `core.Box.get`, `core.Box<int>.get`, `core.Engine`, `core.Engine.start`, and `Vec.operator[]`
  - `DECL_METHOD(Greeter, ping)` recovers `Greeter.ping` with `macro_generated=True`
  - `DECL_COMPLEX(Greeter, pong, int)` does not recover `Greeter.pong`, matching the published known-gap boundary
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_extracts_c_function_and_type_symbols tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_extracts_c_callback_returning_function_pointer tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_skips_c_function_pointer_variables tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_extracts_cpp_class_and_qualified_method_symbols tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_extracts_cpp_namespace_template_and_operator_symbols tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_recovers_cpp_macro_generated_method_symbols tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py::test_build_language_support_contract_reports_frontend_extension_scope tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py::test_parser_capability_check_returns_required_and_known_gap_counts tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py::test_parser_capability_check_promotes_js_assignment_cases_to_required_passes`
  - result: `9 passed in 1.80s`
- History/provenance clue:
  - the parser/support-contract landing visible in git history is commit `370abc7` (`feat(parsers): add baseline C/C++ symbol extraction and support contracts`), which touches the same parser/support-contract/test/docs surface this issue describes
- Gap between the original low-signal wording and the shipped outcome:
  - the real delivered contract is not generic “close parser gaps”
  - it is a deterministic declarator/fqname/macro-boundary hardening pass with explicit required cases and one remaining narrow macro known gap

title: Add support for C and C++
description:
Current code already ships first-class C and C++ parser integration rather than a config-only extension toggle. The default parser registry maps `.c` and `.h` to the `c` parser and maps `.cpp`, `.cc`, `.cxx`, `.hpp`, `.hh`, and `.hxx` to the `cpp` parser, so C-family files participate in indexing, symbol extraction, parser diagnostics, and search without repo-specific overrides.

The shipped scope is broader than the original one-line issue suggested. C support includes function definitions and declarations, struct/union and enum extraction, and special handling for callback-returning function-pointer declarators while explicitly avoiding false positives for plain function-pointer variables. C++ support includes class/struct and enum extraction, class-body method declarations/definitions, qualified out-of-class method owners, namespace-qualified container fqnames, template-specialized method fqnames, operator-method normalization, and bounded recovery for strict macro-generated method patterns.

This issue is therefore best understood as the foundational C/C++ language-support landing, not the final word on every C++ construct. A later discovered-from follow-up (`bd-ygl`) tightened remaining declarator and macro edges, so the current repo state reflects the base feature plus one gap-closure pass. The still-published C++ boundary today is explicit: macro-generated symbols outside strict placeholder patterns remain a known gap.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py`
  - builtin extension mapping now treats `.c`/`.h` as `c` and `.cpp`/`.cc`/`.cxx`/`.hpp`/`.hh`/`.hxx` as `cpp`.
  - parser instances are still the shared `TreeSitterParser(...)` adapter path, not bespoke C/C++ adapters.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py`
  - `_LANGUAGE_SPECS` adds `c` and `cpp` with C-family declaration/function nodes and C++ class/field/declaration nodes.
  - `_c_family_declarator_info(...)` and related declarator walkers are the core logic for separating callable declarators from plain function-pointer variables.
  - `_cpp_namespace_container(...)`, `_cpp_has_qualified_method_declarator(...)`, and `_cpp_method_container_from_qualified_declarator(...)` are the owner-resolution path for namespace-qualified and out-of-class method fqnames.
  - `_collect_cpp_macro_patterns(...)` plus `_CppMacroPattern` implement the intentionally narrow macro-recovery contract for bounded placeholder forms.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py`
  - publishes C baseline constructs (`function_definition`, `function_declaration`, `struct_union_declaration`, `enum_declaration`, `function_pointer_declarator`).
  - publishes C++ baseline constructs (`class_struct_declaration`, `class_body_method_definition`, `class_body_method_declaration`, `qualified_method_definition`, `namespace_qualified_container_fqname`, `template_operator_normalization`, `macro_generated_recoverable_patterns`) and one explicit known gap (`macro_generated_complex_forms`).
  - parser-check corpus includes dedicated C and C++ cases that lock the behavior.
- `tests/unit/test_parsers.py` and `tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py`
  - cover extension routing, C declarator behavior, C++ fqname/method normalization, macro recovery, and published support-contract shape.
- docs
  - `README.md` and `docs/QUICKSTART.md` list C/C++ in the default supported language/extension set and point users to `parsers check --json` when symbol fidelity matters.

Recommended scope interpretation:
- treat `bd-phx` as the base language-enablement milestone;
- use `bd-ygl` for the later declarator/fqname/macro refinement story instead of overloading this issue to mean “all C++ parser edges are solved”.
acceptance_criteria:
- Default support includes `.c`, `.h`, `.cpp`, `.cc`, `.cxx`, `.hpp`, `.hh`, and `.hxx` with builtin parser routing and no operator-specific parser overrides required.
- C extraction yields functions, declarations, structs/unions, and enums, while callback-returning function-pointer declarators are recognized as callable and plain function-pointer variables are not misclassified as functions.
- C++ extraction yields classes/structs, enums, in-class and out-of-class methods, namespace-qualified owner fqnames, template-specialized method fqnames, and operator-method names.
- Strictly recoverable macro-generated C++ method forms are extracted, while more complex macro-generated forms remain an explicit known gap rather than silent or partial behavior.
- `gloggur parsers check --json`, the published support contract, tests, and user-facing docs all reflect the final C/C++ support scope.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/parsers/registry.py` maps:
  - `.c`, `.h` -> `c`
  - `.cpp`, `.cc`, `.cxx`, `.hpp`, `.hh`, `.hxx` -> `cpp`
- Direct registry probe on 2026-03-26 confirmed those mappings for `sample.c`, `sample.h`, `sample.cpp`, `sample.hpp`, `sample.cc`, `sample.cxx`, `sample.hh`, and `sample.hxx`.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py:_LANGUAGE_SPECS` currently declares:
  - `c`: `function_definition`, `declaration`, `struct_specifier`, `union_specifier`, `enum_specifier`
  - `cpp`: `function_definition`, `declaration`, `field_declaration`, `class_specifier`, `struct_specifier`, `enum_specifier`
- `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py` publishes:
  - C baseline: function definitions/declarations, struct/union declarations, enum declarations, function-pointer declarators
  - C++ baseline: class/struct declarations, class-body method definitions/declarations, qualified method definitions, namespace-qualified container fqnames, template/operator normalization, recoverable macro-generated patterns
  - C known gaps: none
  - C++ known gap: `macro-generated symbols outside strict placeholder patterns are not extracted`
- `scripts/gloggur parsers check --json` currently reports `ok: true`, `required_case_counts.passed: 24`, and passing required C/C++ cases for:
  - `c.functions_and_types`
  - `c.callback_returning_function_pointer`
  - `c.function_pointer_variable_not_callable`
  - `cpp.class_and_qualified_methods`
  - `cpp.namespace_qualified_methods`
  - `cpp.template_and_operator_methods`
  - `cpp.macro_generated_method_recovery`
- `tests/unit/test_parsers.py` currently locks the concrete behaviors:
  - C function/type extraction
  - callback-returning function-pointer detection
  - skipping plain function-pointer variables
  - C++ qualified method owner recovery
  - namespace/template/operator fqname normalization
  - bounded macro-generated method recovery
- Direct parser probe on 2026-03-26 confirmed the strict macro boundary:
  - `DECL_METHOD(Greeter, ping)` is recovered as `Greeter.ping`
  - a more complex placeholder form `DECL_COMPLEX(Greeter, pong, int value)` is not recovered, matching the published known gap boundary
- `README.md` already lists C and C++ in the default supported language set and documents the current C++ macro limits as a known fidelity boundary.
- Follow-up context:
  - `bd-ygl` was discovered from this issue and is already closed, so current repo behavior around namespace-qualified/container fqnames, template/operator normalization, and bounded macro recovery is stronger than the original one-line `bd-phx` wording implied.
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_parser_registry_supports_known_extensions tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_extracts_c_function_and_type_symbols tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_extracts_c_callback_returning_function_pointer tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_skips_c_function_pointer_variables tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_extracts_cpp_class_and_qualified_method_symbols tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_extracts_cpp_namespace_template_and_operator_symbols tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_recovers_cpp_macro_generated_method_symbols tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py::test_build_language_support_contract_reports_frontend_extension_scope tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py::test_parser_capability_check_returns_required_and_known_gap_counts`
  - result: `9 passed in 1.79s`
- Gap between the original short issue text and current shipped behavior:
  - the original wording only said “parsing and symbol extraction support”;
  - the actual shipped contract includes concrete extension routing, declarator semantics, fqname normalization rules, parser-check corpus coverage, and an explicit macro-generated C++ gap boundary.

title: [F10] Make Inspect Output Actionable by Default
description:
Source: TODOs.md (F10)
Imported Markdown status: ready_for_review
Imported Markdown priority: P0

## F10 - Make Inspect Output Actionable by Default

**Status**: ready_for_review
**Priority**: P0
**Owner**: codex

**Problem**
- Current forced inspect output is too noisy (`618` warnings) to be triaged efficiently.
- Most warnings come from `tests/` and `scripts/`, which obscures production issues in `src/`.

**Goal**
- Make `inspect` produce high-signal output by default and preserve full-audit mode as opt-in.

**Scope**
- Add first-class path-class filters in `inspect` output and CLI options:
  - default focus on `src/`
  - explicit include switches for `tests/` and `scripts/`
- Add grouped warning summaries in JSON payload:
  - by warning type
  - by top file offenders
  - by path class (`src/tests/scripts`)
- Keep backward compatibility for existing fields while adding summary fields.

**Out of Scope**
- Changing parser behavior or embedding model selection.
- Automatically rewriting docstrings.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- `gloggur inspect . --json` returns source-focused output by default (no implicit `tests/` + `scripts/` flood).
- `gloggur inspect . --json --include-tests --include-scripts` preserves full-audit behavior.
- JSON payload includes deterministic grouped summary sections for triage automation.
- Existing consumers of legacy fields (`warnings`, `reports`, `total`) continue to work.

**Tests Required**
- Unit tests for new filter flags and default scope behavior.
- Unit tests for grouped summary payload shape and counts.
- Integration tests validating source-only default and full-audit opt-in.

**Links**
- PR/commit/issues/docs: pending local implementation in this worktree

**Progress Update (2026-02-26)**
- Added first-class inspect scope controls in `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`:
  - new CLI flags: `--include-tests` and `--include-scripts`.
  - default directory traversal now excludes `tests/` + `scripts/` noise while retaining `src/` (and non-tests/scripts paths) for high-signal output.
  - explicit path intent is preserved: inspecting `tests/` or `scripts/` directly still includes those classes without requiring flags.
- Added deterministic warning summaries in inspect JSON payload (backward-compatible legacy fields retained):
  - `inspect_scope` with effective include toggles.
  - `warning_summary.by_warning_type`.
  - `warning_summary.by_path_class` and `warning_summary.reports_by_path_class`.
  - `warning_summary.top_files` (stable sort by warning count desc, then path).
- Added deterministic traversal ordering (`dirs.sort()` / `files.sort()`) to reduce output jitter across runs.
- Added unit coverage in `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py` for:
  - path-class classification and default filter behavior.
  - warning summary grouping/counting for type + path-class + top-file shapes.
- Added integration coverage in `tests/integration/test_cli.py`:
  - verifies `inspect` default source focus (`tests/scripts` excluded).
  - verifies `--include-tests --include-scripts` restores full-audit behavior.
  - verifies explicit `inspect tests/` path remains included (avoids accidental over-filtering on intentional test-only audits).
  - adds payload-schema regression assertions for `inspect_scope` + `warning_summary` keys/types to keep automation contracts stable.
- Added explicit payload contract versioning:
  - `inspect --json` now emits `inspect_payload_schema_version` (initial value: `"1"`).
  - schema regression coverage now asserts presence and value of the schema version marker.
- Inverted failure-mode insight applied:
  - hardened against a fail-open triage mode where test/tooling warnings drown production issues by requiring explicit opt-in for test/script classes in directory scans.
  - hardened against silent automation breakage from JSON shape drift by asserting deterministic summary-schema fields and value types.
- Remaining closure gaps:
  - define and document schema-version bump policy (what changes require incrementing `inspect_payload_schema_version`).

**Progress Update (2026-02-27, schema bump policy + fail-closed inspect failure contract)**
- Closed the remaining schema-policy gap for inspect payloads in `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`:
  - added machine-readable `inspect_payload_schema_policy` with explicit bump semantics.
  - policy now states `inspect_payload_schema_version` must increment on breaking changes:
    - remove/rename existing fields,
    - change existing field type,
    - change existing field semantics,
    - remove/rename existing failure reason codes.
  - policy explicitly allows additive changes without version bumps:
    - adding optional fields,
    - adding new failure reason codes.
- Hardened inspect failure observability to eliminate silent partial failures:
  - inspect JSON now always carries deterministic failure-contract fields:
    - `failure_codes` (stable sorted list),
    - `failure_guidance` (reason-code keyed remediation),
    - `allow_partial` and `allow_partial_applied`.
  - added inspect-specific remediation map for `decode_error`, `read_error`, `parser_unavailable`, and `parse_error`.
  - `failure_codes`/`failure_guidance` are now present even on clean runs (`[]` / `{}`) for branch-safe consumers.
- Added and expanded regression coverage:
  - unit (`tests/unit/test_cli_main.py`):
    - inspect failure-contract normalization test,
    - inspect schema-policy contract-shape test.
  - integration (`tests/integration/test_cli.py`):
    - partial inspect with decode failure emits deterministic failure contract and remains explicit with `allow_partial_applied=true`,
    - non-partial inspect exits non-zero on decode failure (fail-closed),
    - schema-stability test now asserts `inspect_payload_schema_policy`, `allow_partial*`, and empty failure-contract fields on clean runs.
- Documentation updated:
  - `README.md` inspect section now documents schema bump policy and partial-failure contract fields.
- Strange implementation flagged and fixed:
  - `inspect --json` previously emitted `failed_reasons` but omitted `failure_codes`/`failure_guidance`, while other CLI surfaces (`index`/`watch status`) already exposed deterministic failure contracts.
  - this inconsistency could lead automation to treat partial failures as success due to missing machine-readable error taxonomy; inspect now follows the same fail-closed contract style.
- Remaining closure gaps:
  - none for this F10 schema-policy/failure-contract sub-scope; continue follow-on work under F11 for docstring hotspot reduction.

**Progress Update (2026-02-28, unchanged-file cache reuse to prevent false-clean inspect output)**
- Fixed a silent false-green in repeated inspect runs:
  - `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
    - unchanged files now reuse cached audit warning rows instead of contributing nothing to the payload,
    - added `_load_cached_inspect_warning_reports(...)` to rehydrate warning reports from audit cache keyed by file-path prefix,
    - inspect JSON now reports:
      - `cached_files_reused`
      - `cached_warning_reports_reused`
- Added cache support for file-scoped audit warning reuse:
  - `src/gloggur/indexer/cache.py`
    - added `list_audit_warnings_for_file(path)` to enumerate cached audit rows for one source file deterministically.
- Regression coverage added:
  - unit:
    - `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py`
      - cached inspect warning rehydration helper returns stable warning reports for unchanged files.
  - integration:
    - `tests/integration/test_cli.py`
      - second `inspect --json` run without `--force` now preserves the same actionable warning set from cache,
      - verifies `skipped_files`, `cached_files_reused`, and `cached_warning_reports_reused` are explicit in the payload.
- Documentation update:
  - `README.md` now states that unchanged inspect runs reuse cached warning reports rather than returning a false-clean payload.
- Inverted failure-mode analysis:
  - before this change, `inspect` skipped unchanged files but did not rehydrate cached warnings, so a second run could report zero warnings even when the first run had actionable findings.
  - that was a classic silent failure: the command looked healthy and deterministic while actually erasing signal from the operator view.
  - now unchanged-file reuse is explicit and machine-readable.
- Strange implementation flagged and fixed:
  - inspect cached audit warnings but previously ignored them on the next unchanged run, effectively treating “skip work” as “no findings”.
  - fixed by making skip-path reuse explicit in payloads and tests.
- Remaining closure gap:
  - cached warning reuse currently rehydrates warning-bearing reports only; non-warning semantic score metadata for unchanged files is still not persisted separately, so deeper historical score introspection remains out of scope for this task slice.

**Progress Update (2026-02-28, full inspect-report reuse + stale audit row pruning)**
- Closed the remaining F10 cache-reuse gap in `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` and `src/gloggur/indexer/cache.py`:
  - inspect now reuses full cached audit reports for unchanged files, not just warning-bearing rows.
  - clean semantic-score reports now survive repeated `inspect --json` runs via structured audit payload reuse.
  - new payload field `cached_reports_reused` makes this explicit alongside `cached_files_reused` and `cached_warning_reports_reused`.
- Hardened stale-report cleanup to fail closed on fixed files:
  - fresh inspect runs now clear cached audit rows for each processed file before writing current reports.
  - this prevents previously fixed warnings from being resurrected on the next unchanged inspect run.
- Added backward-compatible structured audit persistence:
  - audit rows now support legacy warning-only payloads and structured report payloads (`warnings`, `semantic_score`, `score_metadata`) under the same cache table.
  - legacy readers still obtain warning lists, while inspect can rehydrate full reports for unchanged files.
- Added regression coverage:
  - unit:
    - `tests/unit/test_cache.py::test_cache_round_trip_structured_audit_reports_and_legacy_warning_reads`
    - `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_load_cached_inspect_reports_rehydrates_cached_reports`
  - integration:
    - `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_inspect_reuses_cached_clean_reports_for_unchanged_files`
    - `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_inspect_clears_stale_cached_reports_after_file_is_fixed`
    - revalidated existing unchanged-warning reuse coverage.
- Inverted failure-mode analysis:
  - before this change, repeated inspect runs silently dropped clean semantic-score metadata for unchanged files because only warning-bearing cache rows were rehydrated.
  - worse, fixed files could still have stale cached warning rows for removed/changed symbols, so a later unchanged run could resurrect obsolete findings even though the force-reinspect run was clean.
  - both paths are now blocked by full-report reuse plus per-file audit-row replacement.
- Strange implementation flagged and fixed:
  - inspect cached audit rows append-only by symbol id and never cleared them per file on fresh reinspection, which is a classic stale-state footgun once unchanged-run cache reuse exists.
  - fixed by replacing cached audit rows per processed file and by reusing full structured report payloads instead of warnings-only slices.
- Verification evidence:
  - `source ./.venv/bin/activate && ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -n 0 tests/unit/test_cache.py tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_load_cached_inspect_reports_rehydrates_cached_reports tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_inspect_reuses_cached_warning_reports_for_unchanged_files tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_inspect_reuses_cached_clean_reports_for_unchanged_files tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_inspect_clears_stale_cached_reports_after_file_is_fixed -q` (`15 passed`)
  - `source ./.venv/bin/activate && ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -n 0 tests/unit/test_cli_main.py -q -k 'inspect' tests/integration/test_cli.py -q -k 'inspect'` (`inspect-focused suite passed`)
- Remaining closure gap:
  - none for this F10 cache-reuse sub-scope.

**DONE Candidate (2026-02-28)**
**Delivered**
- Made `inspect` source-focused by default with explicit `--include-tests` and `--include-scripts` opt-ins.
- Added stable grouped warning summaries, schema policy metadata, and fail-closed inspect failure contracts.
- Fixed repeated-run false-cleans by reusing cached reports and pruning stale audit rows per file.

**Verification**
- `source ./.venv/bin/activate && ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -n 0 tests/unit/test_cache.py tests/unit/test_cli_main.py -q -k 'inspect' tests/integration/test_cli.py -q -k 'inspect'` (`inspect-focused suite passed`)
- `source ./.venv/bin/activate && GLOGGUR_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER='' scripts/gloggur inspect src/gloggur --json --force --allow-partial` (`warning_summary.by_path_class.src = 122`; `tests/scripts = 0`; failure contract fields present)

**Evidence**
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
- `src/gloggur/indexer/cache.py`
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py`
- `README.md`

**Remaining External Evidence**
- None

title: [F6] Incremental Rebuild Engine for Changed Files Only
description:
Source: TODOs.md (F6)
Imported Markdown status: ready_for_review
Imported Markdown priority: P0

## F6 - Incremental Rebuild Engine for Changed Files Only

**Status**: ready_for_review
**Priority**: P0
**Owner**: codex

**Problem**
- Full reindexing penalizes iterative workflows and undermines agent turnaround in ephemeral environments.
- Small file changes currently force disproportionately expensive index rebuild paths.

**Goal**
- Recompute only the symbols affected by changed files while preserving deterministic index correctness.

**Scope**
- Introduce change-set detection keyed by file content digest + symbol extraction result.
- Rebuild only impacted file embeddings and metadata records; remove deleted/renamed file symbols safely.
- Add clear CLI observability fields for incremental runs:
  - files scanned
  - files changed
  - symbols added/updated/removed
  - elapsed time
- Add fallback to full rebuild on incompatible cache/profile/schema conditions.

**Out of Scope**
- Filesystem watcher redesign beyond existing watch-mode behavior.
- Parallel/distributed indexing across hosts.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Re-running `index` on unchanged workspace performs near-no-op update with explicit “no changes” reporting.
- Single-file edits update only that file’s symbol rows/vectors and preserve search correctness.
- File renames/deletions remove stale symbols with no ghost search hits.
- Incompatible cache states automatically trigger safe full rebuild with explicit reason.

**Tests Required**
- Unit tests for change detection, rename/deletion handling, and no-op behavior.
- Unit tests for vector/metadata consistency after incremental update paths.
- Integration tests comparing incremental vs full rebuild search results on the same fixture repo.
- Performance regression check asserting unchanged-run speedup versus baseline full rebuild.

**Links**
- PR/commit/issues/docs: pending local implementation in this worktree

**Progress Update (2026-02-26)**
- Added stale-file pruning during full `index` runs in `src/gloggur/indexer/indexer.py`:
  - reindex now computes `cached_paths - seen_paths` and removes stale file metadata/symbol rows.
  - vector ids for stale files are removed when vector store is active, preventing lingering vector-only artifacts.
- Added incremental observability counters to index results (`IndexResult.as_payload()`):
  - `files_scanned` (alias of files considered),
  - `files_changed`,
  - `files_removed`,
  - `symbols_added`,
  - `symbols_updated`,
  - `symbols_removed`.
- Added per-file delta accounting in `index_file_with_outcome`:
  - compares previous and current symbol sets/body hashes to classify add/update/remove symbol deltas.
- Added cache helper `list_file_paths()` for deterministic stale-path pruning input.
- Added regression coverage:
  - unit: `tests/unit/test_indexer.py::test_indexer_prunes_deleted_files_and_reports_symbol_removals`.
  - integration: `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_index_reports_incremental_observability_and_prunes_deleted_files`.
  - inverted failure-mode regression (priority scenario #1): `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_index_rename_does_not_leave_ghost_symbols`.
- Inverted failure-mode insight applied:
  - addressed "success reported but wrong index retained" for rename/delete flows where stale old-path symbols could survive and create ghost retrieval candidates.
- Remaining closure gaps:
  - add explicit regression for symbol add/remove mismatches where vector and metadata counts diverge under repeated same-file edits.
  - add regression for docstring-only edits to confirm content-hash path does not falsely classify changes as unchanged.

**Progress Update (2026-02-26, inverted-failure follow-up)**
- Added deterministic stale-cleanup failure signaling in `src/gloggur/indexer/indexer.py`:
  - stale-prune exceptions now produce stable reason code `stale_cleanup_error` in `failed_reasons`.
  - index payload now emits structured `failure_guidance` remediation steps keyed by error code.
  - cleanup failures are counted in `failed` so runs cannot report success when stale rows were not fully cleaned.
- Inverted problem explicitly targeted:
