- Adjacent contract clue: `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py` also asserts `find --json` still reports `contract_version == "find_v1"`, so this fix does not require a contract bump.
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_search_router.py::test_extract_query_hints_marks_punctuation_heavy_single_token_literal_first tests/unit/test_search_router.py::test_extract_query_hints_detects_verbatim_literals tests/unit/test_search_router.py::test_search_router_grep_fixed_string_query_preserves_verbatim_literal tests/unit/test_search_router.py::test_search_router_locator_verbatim_queries_skip_symbol_and_semantic_without_about tests/unit/test_search_router.py::test_search_router_locator_verbatim_queries_allow_semantic_rescue_after_exact_miss tests/unit/test_search_exact_backend.py::test_run_exact_backend_locator_verbatim_query_uses_full_literal_fixed_string tests/unit/test_search_exact_backend.py::test_run_exact_backend_locator_verbatim_query_does_not_fallback_to_fragments_after_miss tests/unit/test_search_exact_backend.py::test_run_exact_backend_locator_literal_first_query_uses_fixed_string_without_fallback tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_find_verbatim_literals_prioritize_exact_hits_over_fragment_noise tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_find_verbatim_literal_miss_returns_no_match_and_about_can_rescue tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_find_url_like_single_token_remains_query_text`
  - result: `16 passed, 3 warnings in 3.24s`

title: Investigate Flask benchmark repo index failure and resume blockage during local verification
description: Discovered while verifying bd-3se0. Running scripts/gloggur index on the Flask benchmark repo with local CodeBERT bootstrap reached indexing but failed with storage_error on 67 files, sample failures in tests showing bare AssertionError, plus vector_metadata_mismatch. A subsequent narrowed rerun against subject_repos/flask/src was then blocked by resume_blocked because the interrupted staged build was incompatible (workspace_path_hash_mismatch, index_target_path_changed). This blocked live benchmark-repo verification of the find recovery fix and looks unrelated to bd-3se0s CLI change. Reproduce, identify the extractor or storage assertion path, and make interrupted-build handling less likely to strand follow-up verification attempts.

title: Recover from missing implicit trailing path guesses in find
description: currently treats a trailing token that looks like a repo path but does not exist as a hard failure, even when the remaining query is still searchable. Implement non-blocking recovery for implicit trailing path guesses, preserve fail-fast behavior for unrecoverable path-only cases and explicit scope flags, surface an additive warning code for recovered searches, and add regression tests covering recovery guidance and dotted non-path query text.

title: Make watch state persistence atomic under concurrent status/daemon access
description: GitHub Actions CI run 23160299303 failed on the required Python 3.13 lane when watch status --json returned an io_failure caused by JSONDecodeError while reading the watch state file. Root cause appears to be truncate-then-write races between watch daemon state writes and status reads. Implement a shared atomic JSON write helper, route both watch-state write paths through it, preserve existing malformed-file read semantics, and add a focused concurrency regression test plus targeted watch-status verification.

title: Restore cli/main.py coverage baseline for access and extract-worker branches
description: Latest CI on main failed the protected coverage gate in scripts/check_coverage_baseline.py because src/gloggur/cli/main.py regressed below the checked-in 90% threshold after recent access/init and extract-worker additive branches landed. Scope: add focused tests for uncovered helper and command-family branches, keep the baseline unchanged unless fresh evidence proves otherwise, and re-run the local verification contract.

title: Restore static quality gate after large-catalog edge-worker changes
description: Full pytest is green except the static quality gate harness now exits non-zero after the large-catalog build_edges worker refactor. Diagnose the Ruff failure surfaced by scripts/run_static_quality_gates.py, fix the underlying lint regression without changing indexing behavior, and rerun the failing harness plus targeted verification. --deps discovered-from:bd-5n6v
close_reason: Completed; fixed Ruff regressions in src/gloggur introduced by the large-catalog edge-worker refactor and reverified static gates plus full pytest (1000 passed).

title: Eliminate large-catalog build_edges IPC without index drift
description: Large Stage 1 edge extraction can become dominated by multiprocessing payload deserialization because build_edges sends the full repo symbol catalog to the extract worker on every file. Preserve indexed output and existing staged-build resume/checkpoint semantics while changing only execution strategy: add a build_edges worker mode control, inline fallback for large catalogs, shared candidate-symbol index priming in the worker, additive diagnostics in index JSON, and benchmark harness env passthrough. Include regression coverage proving symbols/chunks/edges and persisted rows remain identical.
close_reason: Completed; implemented large-catalog build_edges worker fallback and primed-catalog reuse, added JSON diagnostics/env passthrough, and verified with targeted pytest suite (93 passed).

title: Fail loud when gloggur editable install points at a different checkout
description:
This issue is narrower and more concrete than the original “cache churn” sketch. The real failure mode is runtime provenance ambiguity across entrypoints: a repo-local editable environment can resolve `gloggur` from a different checkout or package root than the workspace the operator believes they are running, while current diagnostics primarily report cache/profile state rather than the actual imported module path.

Today the safest repo-local entrypoint is `scripts/gloggur`, which is bootstrap-aware and prepends the current repo import root to `PYTHONPATH` before launching. The riskier path is the installed console script `./.venv/bin/gloggur`, which is just a thin wrapper importing `gloggur.cli.main` directly. In editable installs, that import follows whatever package root the environment exposes, so a stale `.pth` or different worktree install can silently run another checkout's code while still reading this repo's `.gloggur-cache` and support directories.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- `./.venv/bin/gloggur` does not go through the repo bootstrap wrapper or any provenance guard; it imports `gloggur.cli.main` immediately.
- The current editable install metadata in this environment is `.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/__editable__.gloggur-0.1.0.pth`, which points at `/Users/auzi/vinnustofa/gloggur/src` in the healthy case.
- Direct local probes show why provenance must be explicit rather than inferred from one path source:
  - interactive `.venv/bin/python -c 'import gloggur'` from repo root resolves `gloggur` from `/Users/auzi/vinnustofa/gloggur/gloggur/__init__.py` because `cwd` is on `sys.path`
  - simulated `./.venv/bin/gloggur` import order resolves `gloggur` from `/Users/auzi/vinnustofa/gloggur/src/gloggur/__init__.py`
- `status --json` reports cache/index-profile integrity and entrypoint metadata, but it does not report executable path, actual imported package root, or whether those paths match the current repo root.
- Support diagnostics and support-runtime traces similarly record `repo_root`, `cwd`, command args, and bootstrap/status artifacts, but not the actual import root or console-script executable path.
- When those provenance surfaces diverge across worktrees, the resulting symptoms look like repeated cache schema/index-profile rebuild churn with weak diagnostic attribution instead of an explicit environment mismatch failure.

The remaining work is therefore runtime provenance hardening: detect and surface which checkout/package root is actually executing, compare it with the expected repo root, and thread that information through normal status/support diagnostics so stale editable installs fail or warn loudly instead of masquerading as generic cache drift.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `./.venv/bin/gloggur` is currently a plain console script with:
  - shebang to the repo-local venv Python
  - `from gloggur.cli.main import main`
  - no bootstrap or provenance validation layer
- `scripts/gloggur` is the contrasting safer path:
  - computes `REPO_ROOT`
  - exports `PYTHONPATH=${REPO_ROOT}/src[:...]`
  - executes `python -m gloggur.bootstrap_launcher`
- `src/gloggur/bootstrap_launcher.py` already has partial provenance ingredients:
  - `_repo_root(...)` and `_import_root(...)` know the expected repo/import root for the wrapper path
  - `_prepend_pythonpath(...)` normalizes the import root onto `PYTHONPATH`
  - `build_ready_payload(...)` and `build_failure_payload(...)` expose `repo_root`, selected interpreter, probes, and `pythonpath`
  - but they do not report the actual resolved `gloggur` module file or compare it against the expected repo/import root
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_build_status_payload(...)` currently exposes cache/integrity fields such as `cache_dir`, `schema_version`, `entrypoint`, `expected_index_profile`, and `cached_index_profile`, but no executable/import-root provenance fields
- `src/gloggur/support.py` captures `config.redacted.json`, `bootstrap_status.json`, `status.json`, and `watch_status.json`, plus session metadata with `repo_root` and `cwd`, but it does not persist the active executable path or resolved imported package root
- `src/gloggur/support_runtime.py:CommandTraceSession` persists `argv`, `cwd`, and `repo_root` in runtime trace metadata, but again not executable/import-root provenance
- Docs and operator guidance already bias users toward `scripts/gloggur` (`AGENTS.md`, `docs/QUICKSTART.md`, `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`), which suggests the intended safe path exists conceptually even though the raw installed console script remains silent

Recommended implementation shape:
- add one shared provenance helper that captures at least:
  - current repo root
  - executable path / interpreter path
  - actual `gloggur` module file and normalized package root
  - expected import root for the current invocation mode
  - explicit booleans/reason codes for provenance match vs mismatch
- thread that helper into `status`, support bundle diagnostics, and support-runtime metadata
- decide failure policy carefully:
  - direct console-script mismatch should likely fail closed or at minimum emit a high-signal warning code
  - wrapper-based healthy launches should remain quiet
  - the policy should key off actual mismatch, not merely off using `.venv/bin/gloggur`

Important scope clarification:
- this is a runtime-entrypoint/provenance bug, not a cache-schema bug by itself
- the goal is not to forbid editable installs; it is to make the active checkout/package root explicit and mismatches loud
- the bootstrap wrapper already mitigates part of the risk, so the highest-value hardening is around direct console-script execution and diagnostic visibility
acceptance_criteria:
- The CLI can report deterministic runtime provenance including repo root, executable/interpreter path, actual imported `gloggur` package root, and a machine-readable match/mismatch verdict.
- `status --json` includes that provenance payload so cache/index-profile drift can be distinguished from environment-root drift.
- Support bundle diagnostics and support-runtime trace metadata include the same provenance fields, making stale editable installs diagnosable after the fact.
- Direct console-script execution from a mismatched editable install fails or warns loudly with a stable error/warning code instead of appearing as generic cache churn.
- Healthy repo-local wrapper execution (`scripts/gloggur ...`) remains supported without false-positive mismatch failures.
- Tests cover healthy provenance, mismatched import-root scenarios, and support/status payload serialization of the new fields.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- Direct entrypoint probes:
  - `sed -n '1,80p' .venv/bin/gloggur` shows the installed console script is only:
    - shebang to `.venv/bin/python`
    - `from gloggur.cli.main import main`
    - `sys.exit(main())`
  - `sed -n '1,40p' .venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/__editable__.gloggur-0.1.0.pth` shows the editable install currently points at `/Users/auzi/vinnustofa/gloggur/src`
- Local healthy-environment probes:
  - interactive `.venv/bin/python` from repo root resolved `gloggur.__file__` to `/Users/auzi/vinnustofa/gloggur/gloggur/__init__.py` because `cwd` is on `sys.path`
  - a simulated `./.venv/bin/gloggur` import order (with `sys.path[0]` set to `.venv/bin` and repo-root `''` removed) resolved `gloggur.__file__` to `/Users/auzi/vinnustofa/gloggur/src/gloggur/__init__.py`
  - this proves the actual imported package root depends on invocation shape and is not derivable from `repo_root` alone
- Bootstrap wrapper state:
  - `scripts/gloggur` explicitly prepends `${REPO_ROOT}/src` to `PYTHONPATH` and executes `python -m gloggur.bootstrap_launcher`
  - `build_ready_payload(...)` currently emits `selected_interpreter`, `selected_candidate`, `repo_root`, and `pythonpath`, but no actual imported module path
  - direct probe of `build_ready_payload(...)` for `status --json` produced only `repo_root`, `pythonpath`, probe data, interpreter, and bootstrap-state fields
- Status/support omission evidence:
  - live `status --json` top-level keys include cache/integrity fields such as `cache_dir`, `schema_version`, `entrypoint`, `expected_index_profile`, and `cached_index_profile`, but no executable/import-root provenance fields
  - `src/gloggur/support.py` support-session and diagnostics payloads record `repo_root`, `cwd`, `config_path`, bootstrap status, status payload, and watch payload only
  - `src/gloggur/support_runtime.py:CommandTraceSession` metadata records `command_name`, `argv`, `cwd`, `repo_root`, pid, stage, and build state, but not executable/import-root provenance
- Focused regression coverage already present around adjacent surfaces:
  - `tests/unit/test_bootstrap_launcher.py::test_failure_payload_contains_stable_fields`
  - `tests/unit/test_bootstrap_launcher.py::test_main_dry_run_emits_ready_payload_without_exec`
  - `tests/unit/test_support_runtime.py::test_command_trace_session_moves_completed_command_to_recent`
  - `tests/integration/test_support_cli.py::test_support_collect_manual_snapshot_includes_runtime_logs`
- Direct verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_bootstrap_launcher.py::test_failure_payload_contains_stable_fields tests/unit/test_bootstrap_launcher.py::test_main_dry_run_emits_ready_payload_without_exec tests/unit/test_support_runtime.py::test_command_trace_session_moves_completed_command_to_recent tests/integration/test_support_cli.py::test_support_collect_manual_snapshot_includes_runtime_logs`
  - result: `4 passed, 3 warnings in 2.55s`
- Gap between the original low-signal wording and current code:
  - the wrapper path already has partial provenance context (`repo_root`, `pythonpath`, selected interpreter)
  - the missing piece is actual import-root verification and propagation into normal diagnostics
  - the primary silent-risk surface is direct installed-console-script execution, not the documented `scripts/gloggur` workflow

title: Persist access grant marker on ready init/access success
description:
Current code already ships this fix. Before the bug fix, `gloggur init` and `gloggur access grant` could report success for an already-ready repository while still leaving `.gloggur/access_grants.json` absent if `.gloggur` and `.gloggur-cache` already existed and no actual access mutations were needed. That produced a false-ready state: access planning said the repo was ready, but the persisted grant marker that downstream tooling and operators expect was missing.

The shipped behavior now treats the marker file as part of the success contract, not just a side effect of mutating grant application. `apply_access_grant(...)` still persists state for non-ready plans, but already-ready plans now flow through `persist_ready_access_grant_state(...)`, and both `init` and `access grant` call that helper whenever `access_ready=true` but the marker file may be missing.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- Already-ready repos with a missing marker are repaired on successful `init` and `access grant` paths rather than being left in a silent partial state.
- The fast path intentionally preserves the public payload shape: `access_ready` is `true`, `access_result` remains `null`, and no synthetic `applied_actions` payload is introduced when no access mutation was required.
- The repaired marker file uses the same `schema_version = "1"` persisted grant-state payload shape as the mutating `apply_access_grant(...)` path.
- The shell wrapper inherits the same behavior because it delegates directly to the CLI `access grant` command.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/access.py`
  - `apply_access_grant(...)` remains the mutating path for non-ready plans and writes `.gloggur/access_grants.json` through `_write_access_grant_result(...)`.
  - `persist_ready_access_grant_state(...)` is the narrow bug-fix path for this issue: it requires `plan.access_ready`, builds an `AccessGrantResult` with empty `applied_actions` / `blocked_paths` / `manual_actions`, sets `access_ready=true`, and writes the same persisted state-file schema without touching repo permissions.
  - `_write_access_grant_result(...)` centralizes the file format, parent-dir creation, and I/O failure wrapping so the ready fast path and mutating path share one persistence contract.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `_run_access_grant_flow(...)` now short-circuits already-ready plans by calling `persist_ready_access_grant_state(access_plan)` and returning `None` before prompt / `--yes` logic, which preserves the existing `access_result = null` CLI contract on the ready fast path.
  - `_init_impl(...)` has a second explicit repair path for `--no-grant-access`: when the plan is already ready and the user skips the grant flow, it still calls `persist_ready_access_grant_state(...)` so `init` cannot leave a missing marker behind.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py`
  - locks the four regression cases that matter here: ready repo marker repair through `init --yes`, `init --no-grant-access`, `access grant --yes`, and `access grant` without `--yes` in JSON mode.
- `tests/unit/test_access.py`
  - locks the underlying state-file schema and existence contract for the mutating grant path.
- `tests/integration/test_access_wrapper.py`
  - confirms the shell wrapper preserves the same marker-persistence outcome via the CLI.

Important scope clarification:
- this issue is not about broadening access permissions or changing the access plan model;
- it is specifically about making the persisted marker file a mandatory part of every successful `access_ready=true` completion path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Successful `access_ready=true` paths always materialize `.gloggur/access_grants.json`, even when `.gloggur` / `.gloggur-cache` already exist and no chmod/mkdir work is needed.
- `gloggur init` repairs the missing marker for already-ready repos both when access is auto-approved and when `--no-grant-access` is used.
- `gloggur access grant` repairs the missing marker for already-ready repos without requiring an access mutation.
- The ready fast path keeps the existing CLI contract shape: `access_ready=true`, `access_result=null`, and no incomplete-access error.
- The persisted state file for the repaired marker remains schema-compatible with the normal grant path (`schema_version = "1"`).
- The shell wrapper path continues to inherit the same success contract through the CLI.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/access.py:176` defines `persist_ready_access_grant_state(...)`, which exists specifically to persist grant state for a plan that is already ready without mutating access permissions.
- `src/gloggur/access.py:_write_access_grant_result(...)` writes the canonical state payload with `schema_version`, ensuring the ready fast path and normal grant path share one file format.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_run_access_grant_flow(...)` calls `persist_ready_access_grant_state(access_plan)` before any prompt or `--yes` handling when `access_plan.access_ready` is already true.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_init_impl(...)` separately repairs the marker on the `--no-grant-access` branch when `access_plan.access_ready` is true, which closes the ready-repo hole for `init` as well as `access grant`.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py` locks the exact regression surface:
  - `test_init_json_repairs_missing_access_marker_for_ready_repo`
  - `test_init_json_no_grant_access_repairs_missing_access_marker_for_ready_repo`
  - `test_access_grant_json_yes_repairs_missing_access_marker_for_ready_repo`
  - `test_access_grant_json_without_yes_repairs_missing_access_marker_for_ready_repo`
- Those tests also confirm the public payload contract on the repaired fast path:
  - `access_ready` is `True`
  - `access_result` stays `None`
  - `.gloggur/access_grants.json` exists after the command returns
- `tests/unit/test_access.py::test_apply_access_grant_creates_missing_gloggur_dirs_and_persists_state` confirms the persisted marker payload includes `schema_version == "1"` and `access_ready == true` on the normal mutating path.
- `tests/integration/test_access_wrapper.py::test_access_wrapper_applies_grant_via_cli` confirms the thin shell wrapper preserves the same marker persistence behavior.
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_access.py::test_apply_access_grant_creates_missing_gloggur_dirs_and_persists_state tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_init_json_repairs_missing_access_marker_for_ready_repo tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_init_json_no_grant_access_repairs_missing_access_marker_for_ready_repo tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_access_grant_json_yes_repairs_missing_access_marker_for_ready_repo tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_access_grant_json_without_yes_repairs_missing_access_marker_for_ready_repo tests/integration/test_access_wrapper.py::test_access_wrapper_applies_grant_via_cli`
  - result: `6 passed in 3.23s`
- Gap between the original short issue text and current shipped behavior:
  - the fix is broader than a single `access grant --yes` path;
  - it covers ready-repo repair through both `init` and `access grant`, including the explicit `--no-grant-access` `init` branch;
  - it deliberately preserves the existing success payload shape instead of fabricating a non-null `access_result` for the repair-only path.

title: Fix static quality gate failure on access grant branch
description: The full pytest run is failing in tests/integration/test_run_static_quality_gates_harness.py because scripts/run_static_quality_gates.py runs Ruff against the full src/gloggur package and reports lint failures. Inspect the concrete Ruff output on this branch, fix the blocking violations if they are in scope, and re-run the static quality harness.

title: Add explicit repo access grant flow to init
description:
Shipped scope is broader and more concrete than the original one-line description suggests. Current code does not just bolt a `--yes` flag onto `gloggur init`; it introduces an explicit conservative repo-access workflow with three public surfaces: `gloggur init`, `gloggur access plan`, and `gloggur access grant`, plus a thin shell wrapper at `scripts/grant_gloggur_access.sh`.

The feature is designed to make repo-local access setup honest and fail-loud without silently rewriting broad repo permissions. `build_access_plan(...)` inspects the repo plus Glöggur-owned state roots (`.gloggur`, `.gloggur-cache`), classifies blocked paths, distinguishes manual OS privacy blockers from normal permission problems, and proposes only Glöggur-local automatic actions. `init` then incorporates that plan into its success/failure contract, while `access grant` applies the narrow local fixes or returns structured incomplete-access diagnostics.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- `gloggur init` always writes repo-local config first, then runs or reports the access workflow instead of pretending initialization is fully complete.
- Default JSON `init` without `--yes` fails non-zero with `access_grant_incomplete` when access is still needed, but still returns the computed `access_plan` so callers can inspect the required actions.
- `init --no-grant-access` succeeds while preserving honesty: it returns `initialized=true`, `access_ready=false`, `access_result=null`, and the unresolved access plan instead of mutating access implicitly.
- `access plan` is a pure inspection surface, while `access grant` is the mutating surface.
- Successful grant paths persist state to `.gloggur/access_grants.json`, and the wrapper script simply delegates to the CLI `access grant` flow.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/access.py`
  - `build_access_plan(...)` is the feature's planner. It scans repo readability/traversability, evaluates `.gloggur` and `.gloggur-cache`, returns deterministic `required_access`, `automatic_actions`, `blocked_paths`, `manual_actions`, and readiness booleans, and marks macOS privacy blockers separately from generic permission failures.
  - `apply_access_grant(...)` is the mutating path. It creates/repairs `.gloggur` and `.gloggur-cache`, rechecks repo blockers, computes manual-action guidance, and persists the grant result under `.gloggur/access_grants.json`.
  - `persist_ready_access_grant_state(...)` and `_write_access_grant_result(...)` make the persisted marker/state file part of the success contract even when no access mutations are required.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `init` integrates the access workflow into repository initialization.
  - conflicting `--yes` and `--no-grant-access` flags fail with `cli_usage_error`.
  - default JSON/text `init` flows can emit structured/prompted `access_grant_incomplete` outcomes rather than silently succeeding.
  - `_emit_access_incomplete(...)` standardizes the non-zero payload contract with `failure_codes`, `failure_guidance`, and a primary error object.
  - `access plan` exposes the planner directly, and `access grant` reuses the same planner plus `_run_access_grant_flow(...)` to preserve shared semantics.
- `scripts/grant_gloggur_access.sh`
  - is intentionally thin; it delegates directly to `scripts/gloggur access grant ...` so wrapper behavior stays aligned with the CLI contract.
- Docs and error catalog
  - `README.md`, `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`, `docs/QUICKSTART.md`, and `docs/ERROR_CODES.md` document the operator-facing flow and the `access_grant_incomplete` remediation path.

Important scope clarification:
- this feature is about repo-local Glöggur access setup, not general-purpose repo permission management;
- automatic actions are constrained to Glöggur-owned roots and explicit state repair, while unreadable repo files and OS privacy gates remain explicit manual blockers.
acceptance_criteria:
- `gloggur access plan <path>` returns a deterministic access-plan payload with required access, automatic actions, blocked paths, manual actions, and readiness booleans.
- `gloggur init <path> --json` fails non-zero with `access_grant_incomplete` when access work is still required and the caller did not approve the grant flow.
- `gloggur init <path> --no-grant-access --json` remains a truthful non-mutating success path: repo config is written, `access_ready` reflects reality, and callers receive the unresolved access plan.
- `gloggur access grant <path> --yes --json` applies the Glöggur-local fixes, persists `.gloggur/access_grants.json`, and reports `access_ready=true` when no blockers remain.
- Blocked repo files and macOS privacy blockers are surfaced explicitly in plan/result payloads and feed manual-action guidance instead of being silently ignored.
- The wrapper script `scripts/grant_gloggur_access.sh` preserves the same access-grant contract by delegating directly to the CLI.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/access.py:build_access_plan(...)` is the core planner and already ships the detailed access contract:
  - repo-root plus Glöggur-owned state requirements under `required_access`
  - automatic actions for `.gloggur` / `.gloggur-cache`
  - `blocked_paths` with structured reasons/details
  - `manual_os_action_required` when darwin privacy blockers are detected
- `tests/unit/test_access.py` locks key planner behaviors:
  - `test_build_access_plan_is_ready_when_repo_and_gloggur_paths_are_writable`
  - `test_build_access_plan_reports_blocked_repo_file`
  - `test_build_access_plan_marks_macos_privacy_blockers`
  - these confirm both the ready path and the blocker classification path.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` shows the user-facing integration contract:
  - `init` rejects conflicting `--yes` and `--no-grant-access`
  - default JSON `init` without approval returns a structured `access_grant_incomplete` failure
  - `init --no-grant-access` succeeds without mutating access and still returns the access plan
  - `access plan` is pure inspection
  - `access grant` is the mutating path
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py` locks those exact CLI semantics:
  - `test_init_json_rejects_conflicting_access_flags`
  - `test_init_json_without_yes_returns_access_plan_only_and_fails`
  - `test_init_json_no_grant_access_preserves_success`
  - `test_access_plan_json_returns_deterministic_plan`
  - `test_access_grant_json_without_yes_returns_incomplete_payload`
  - `test_access_grant_json_yes_applies_and_persists_result`
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_emit_access_incomplete(...)` and `docs/ERROR_CODES.md` publish the explicit machine-readable failure code `access_grant_incomplete` plus remediation guidance.
- `scripts/grant_gloggur_access.sh` is intentionally only a delegate to `scripts/gloggur access grant`, and `tests/integration/test_access_wrapper.py::test_access_wrapper_applies_grant_via_cli` confirms it preserves the same success behavior and marker persistence.
- `README.md` and `docs/QUICKSTART.md` already describe the intended operator flow: use `init --yes` to apply Glöggur-local fixes during setup, or `access plan` / the wrapper script when running the access flow separately.
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_access.py::test_build_access_plan_is_ready_when_repo_and_gloggur_paths_are_writable tests/unit/test_access.py::test_build_access_plan_reports_blocked_repo_file tests/unit/test_access.py::test_build_access_plan_marks_macos_privacy_blockers tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_init_json_without_yes_returns_access_plan_only_and_fails tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_init_json_no_grant_access_preserves_success tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_access_plan_json_returns_deterministic_plan tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_access_grant_json_without_yes_returns_incomplete_payload tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_access_grant_json_yes_applies_and_persists_result tests/integration/test_access_wrapper.py::test_access_wrapper_applies_grant_via_cli`
  - result: `9 passed in 3.30s`
- Gap between the original short issue text and current shipped behavior:
  - the feature is broader than "init gained an access grant flow"
  - the shipped contract includes separate plan/apply commands, honest failure guidance, wrapper support, blocker classification, and persisted grant state
  - the design is intentionally conservative: it repairs Glöggur-local state but leaves broader repo/OS permission blockers explicit and manual

title: Refactor staged cursor resume to use canonical staged tables
description: Replace the duplicate checkpoint-payload resume implementation with a low-overhead design that keeps only per-file checkpoint summaries and uses the staged cache's canonical files/symbols/chunks/edges tables as the durable source of truth for extract_complete and embedded_complete resume state. Preserve resume counts/ranking and publish cleanup, and add regression coverage for extract skip, embed resume, and checkpoint cleanup.

title: Persist per-file extract checkpoints for staged-build resume
description: Implement true cursor resume for staged index builds by persisting per-file extract checkpoints in the staged cache, stat-validating them on resume, replaying only drifted/new work, and rebuilding the embed queue from extract-complete checkpoint rows. Cover cache/indexer/CLI contracts and resume recovery tests.

title: Fail fast on extract_symbols stalls during resumed index
description: Interrupted/resumed index runs can stall indefinitely in the extract_symbols stage with no file-level progress surfaced in build-state, status, or support traces. Add additive extract progress metadata, fail-fast timeout handling around per-file prepare/edge extraction, and structured failure contracts so interrupted staged builds remain resumable and diagnosable. Validate with synthetic hang fixtures and, when possible, a live Gemini resume repro under betatester support.

title: Fix resume/ledger CLI regressions in test doubles and JSON contracts
description: Regression cleanup after bd-0f7. The interrupted-build resume and embedding-ledger change introduced failures in unrelated CLI and verification surfaces because older/fake cache objects used in tests do not implement the new staged-build resume APIs, and additive search/index JSON fields appear to be handled as required in some call paths. Scope: restore backward-compatible behavior for cache test doubles, keep new recovery metadata additive, and get the affected CLI/static-quality tests green without forcing a reindex.

title: Resume interrupted index builds and reuse cached embedding batches
description: Implement resumable interrupted staged-cache indexing plus a durable embedding dedupe ledger so interrupted Gemini indexing can continue without re-embedding already-paid tokens. Scope includes: auto-resume compatible interrupted staged builds; explicit legacy adoption for pre-update interrupted staged caches; additive status/search/index JSON recovery metadata; and clear-cache semantics that preserve the embedding ledger by default with an opt-in purge flag. Acceptance requires coverage for manifest compatibility, legacy adoption, interrupted resume, ledger reuse, and no forced reindex solely due to shipping the feature.

title: Recover remaining Express-style JS dynamic-key member factories and contextual descriptor owners
description:
Current code already shipped most of the JavaScript recovery work implied by this issue. The tree-sitter parser now recovers assignment alias chains, export-root aliases, identifier-shaped string-literal subscript assignments, exact `Object.defineProperty(...)` descriptor entries with literal keys, object-literal methods, and alias-propagated object owners. The remaining issue is narrower: syntactically recoverable computed keys and contextual owners still drop out of the symbol table, so Express-style factory patterns continue to undercount.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- The published JavaScript support contract already treats `string_literal_subscript_assignment`, `define_property_descriptor`, `object_binding_property`, and `object_binding_alias_propagation` as baseline, and the parser-check corpus passes those cases today.
- `TreeSitterParser._member_expression_path(...)` only resolves `identifier`, `member_expression`, and `subscript_expression` when the subscript index is an identifier-shaped quoted string via `_identifier_string_property_name(...)`. It does not resolve identifier-backed computed keys or `this`.
- As a result, `app["all"] = fn` extracts `app.all`, but `const method = "all"; app[method] = fn`, `methods.forEach(function (method) { app[method] = fn; })`, and similar enumerated member factories emit no symbols at all. There is no current constant propagation or enumerable key expansion.
- `_define_property_symbol_specs(...)` only extracts `Object.defineProperty(...)` descriptors when the owner argument resolves through `_member_expression_path(...)` and the property key is a quoted identifier string. `Object.defineProperty(res, "connection", ...)` works, but `Object.defineProperty(this, "router", ...)` and `Object.defineProperty(app, key, ...)` emit nothing.
- Because `this` is not a recoverable owner path today, direct `this.router = function () {}` and `this["router"] = function () {}` also emit no symbols. The contextual-owner problem is therefore broader than descriptors alone.
- The currently published JavaScript known gaps only mention `computed_identifier_subscript_assignment` and `helper_runtime_mutation`; the contextual-owner / identifier-descriptor-key gap is real in code today but is not yet named separately in the support contract.

The remaining work is therefore not a general “Express still bad” bucket. It is a scoped parser-contract follow-up: recover statically inferable computed-key member bindings and define a fail-closed policy for contextual owners such as `this`, while keeping truly dynamic helper/runtime mutation explicit as a known gap.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py`
  - `_synthetic_symbol_specs(...)` is the JS-family entrypoint that decides whether a function/class expression becomes a synthetic assignment-bound symbol.
  - `_assignment_symbol_targets(...)`, `_binding_target_nodes_for_value(...)`, `_assignment_symbol_target(...)`, and `_assignment_symbol_targets_for_node(...)` currently recover identifier/member targets and alias-expanded owners, but only after `_member_expression_path(...)` succeeds.
  - `_member_expression_path(...)` is the critical gate for this issue. Today it supports:
    - bare identifiers
    - dotted member chains
    - subscript expressions whose index is an identifier-shaped quoted string
    It does not support `this`, identifier-valued subscripts, or any constrained constant/evaluable key resolution.
  - `_define_property_symbol_specs(...)` requires both a recoverable owner path and a quoted identifier property key, which is why `Object.defineProperty(res, "connection", ...)` works while `Object.defineProperty(this, "router", ...)` and `Object.defineProperty(app, key, ...)` do not.
  - `_collect_symbols(...)` already threads `current_container` through traversal, so there is existing context available if the repo chooses to map `this` onto a stable container fqname in narrowly defined situations. That suggests the contextual-owner work can stay local to parser ownership resolution rather than requiring a new traversal architecture.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py`
  - the current contract already publishes `computed_identifier_subscript_assignment` and `helper_runtime_mutation` as JavaScript known gaps;
  - this issue should either extend baseline coverage for a narrow set of computed-key/contextual-owner forms or add explicit new known-gap language for the still-unsupported contextual-owner cases instead of leaving them undocumented.
- `tests/unit/test_parsers.py`
  - existing JS regression coverage already locks the landed alias-chain, literal-subscript, descriptor, and object-binding behavior;
  - new coverage belongs here for any accepted constant-key / enumerable-factory / contextual-owner rules.
- `tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py`
  - publish any new construct tier or known-gap wording here so `parsers check --json` and contract consumers stay aligned with actual parser behavior.

Recommended scope boundaries:
- acceptable recovery targets: single local const-string keys, trivially enumerable local arrays/tuples of identifier-shaped strings, and contextual owners whose fqname can be derived deterministically from the current container or explicit owner path;
- explicit non-goals unless separately justified: helper-driven runtime mutation, opaque callback dataflow, arbitrary computed expressions, or emitting raw `this.router` fqnames when the owning container is ambiguous.
acceptance_criteria:
- Existing JavaScript baseline cases for alias chains, literal string subscript assignments, defineProperty descriptors with literal keys, and object-binding aliases remain passing.
- Statically recoverable identifier-backed computed key assignments extract symbols when the key domain is singular or trivially enumerable (for example a local const string or an immediate enumerable of identifier-shaped strings), or they remain explicit known-gap cases with dedicated contract/test coverage.
- Contextual owners such as `this` follow an explicit fail-closed policy: recover them only when a stable owner fqname can be derived deterministically from parser context, and do not emit ambiguous bare `this.*` fqnames.
- `Object.defineProperty(...)` with contextual owners and non-literal-but-statically-resolvable keys is either recovered under that same deterministic policy or published as an explicit known gap.
- The JavaScript support contract and `parsers check --json` corpus include positive and/or known-gap coverage for computed identifier subscripts, contextual owners, and descriptor-key resolution so the published contract matches real parser behavior.
- Helper-driven runtime mutation remains out of scope unless separately promoted from known gap to baseline.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_recovers_remaining_javascript_binding_symbols` already proves the landed scope:
  - alias-chain member assignments are recovered
  - `app["all"] = fn` is recovered as `app.all`
  - `Object.defineProperty(res, "connection", ...)` descriptor symbols are recovered
  - `const method = "all"; app[method] = fn` is intentionally still missing (`assert "app.miss" not in grouped`)
- `scripts/gloggur parsers check --json` currently publishes the JavaScript contract as:
  - baseline: `string_literal_subscript_assignment`, `define_property_descriptor`, `object_binding_property`, `object_binding_alias_propagation`
  - known gap: `computed_identifier_subscript_assignment`, `helper_runtime_mutation`
  - there is currently no separate published gap/case for contextual `this` owners or identifier descriptor keys
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py:_member_expression_path(...)` only resolves identifier/member chains plus subscript indices accepted by `_identifier_string_property_name(...)`, which is why identifier-backed computed keys are dropped today.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/treesitter_parser.py:_define_property_symbol_specs(...)` requires:
  - `Object.defineProperty` as the exact callee path
  - a recoverable owner path from argument 0
  - an identifier-shaped quoted string property key from argument 1
- Direct parser probes on 2026-03-26 confirmed current residual behavior:
  - `methods.forEach(function (method) { app[method] = function wrapped() {}; })` -> no symbols
  - `const method = "all"; app[method] = function wrapped() {};` -> no symbols
  - `Object.defineProperty(this, "router", { value: function router() {} })` -> no symbols
  - `Object.defineProperty(app, key, { value: function router() {} })` -> no symbols
  - `this.router = function router() {};` -> no symbols
  - `this["router"] = function router() {};` -> no symbols
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_parsers.py::test_treesitter_parser_recovers_remaining_javascript_binding_symbols tests/unit/test_parser_support_contract.py::test_build_language_support_contract_reports_frontend_extension_scope`
  - result: `2 passed in 1.68s`
- Gap between the original low-signal wording and current shipped behavior:
  - the prior JS recovery pass already landed most of the syntactically straightforward Express/CommonJS cases
  - the remaining issue is specifically constrained constant/computed-key recovery plus contextual-owner policy
  - the support contract currently under-describes the contextual-owner/identifier-descriptor-key gap even though code probes show it is real

title: Recover remaining Express-style JS symbols from alias chains, literal subscripts, and defineProperty descriptors
description: Follow-up discovered from bd-j7x after local Express benchmarking still showed material undercounting. Extend JS/TS/TSX extraction for syntactically recoverable CommonJS/backend patterns: flatten assignment alias chains, support export-root aliases, normalize identifier-shaped string literal subscript assignments, extract exact Object.defineProperty descriptor value/get/set symbols, propagate object-literal member extraction across recoverable aliases, and preserve stable function/method/class kinds with richer metadata. Keep dynamic identifier keys and helper-driven runtime mutation as explicit residual gaps. Add parser/index/search/support-contract/docs regressions and rerun the local Express benchmark to verify symbol count rises materially above 241.

title: Fix false path_not_writable from support-runtime metadata write race
description: gloggur index --json can fail late when support-runtime metadata writes race on a shared temp file (meta.json.tmp) and one writer sees FileNotFoundError during replace. Make support-runtime metadata writes race-safe and fail-open so telemetry failures do not abort command execution; surface additive warning code support_runtime_degraded instead of command-fatal IO payloads.
close_reason: Implemented: race-safe support-runtime metadata writes with fail-open degradation and warning-code propagation

title: Fix embedding breakdown pre-commit hook for src-layout paths
description: The repo pre-commit hook refreshes docs/semantic-search-embedding-breakdown-base.md using hard-coded gloggur/... paths, but the repository uses a src/ layout. Commits fail with 'Source file not found' when the hook tries to inspect gloggur/embeddings/base.py. Update the hook to watch and invoke src/gloggur/... paths, then verify the generator succeeds.

title: Disable graph edge embeddings by default
description:
This issue shipped a narrower and more concrete contract than the title suggests. Current code does not disable graph extraction; it disables only semantic graph-edge embeddings by default. Index runs still build and persist reference-graph edges (`DEFINES`, `IMPORTS`, `CALLS`, `REFERENCES`, `TESTS`, etc.) as structural metadata, but they omit edge text serialization and edge embedding vectors unless the operator explicitly opts in.

In current repo state, the default `GloggurConfig` sets `embed_graph_edges=False`, the index CLI exposes an explicit boolean flag pair `--embed-graph-edges/--no-embed-graph-edges`, config/env can enable the behavior via `embed_graph_edges: true` or `GLOGGUR_EMBED_GRAPH_EDGES=true`, and the active index profile now includes the flag as `|embed_graph_edges=0|1`. That means edge-vector behavior participates in cache/profile compatibility and resume/status reporting instead of being an invisible implementation detail.

The real delivered change is therefore an opt-in cost/fidelity boundary: chunk embeddings remain eager by default, graph structure remains intact, and only edge text/vector generation becomes conditional. When edge embeddings are enabled, the same structural edges gain `EDGE_TYPE:...` text payloads and embedding vectors; when disabled, edge storage remains present but structural-only.

Observed current state as of 2026-03-26:
- Default index runs still persist graph edges and report non-zero `graph_edge_count`, but `embedded_edge_vectors` stays `0`.
- Opt-in runs preserve the same structural edge count while increasing `embedded_edge_vectors` and total embedded vector count.
- Index/profile compatibility now distinguishes `embed_graph_edges=0` vs `embed_graph_edges=1`, while read paths still tolerate legacy cache profiles that predate the suffix when the only mismatch is the missing edge-embedding flag.
- Verbose metrics/reporting separate symbol-chunk versus graph-edge vector coverage instead of collapsing both into one undifferentiated embedding count.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/config.py`
  - `GloggurConfig.embed_graph_edges` now defaults to `False`.
  - `embedding_profile()` includes the option suffix `|embed_graph_edges={0|1}` so cache compatibility, resume metadata, and status payloads track whether edge vectors were enabled.
  - environment loading supports `GLOGGUR_EMBED_GRAPH_EDGES`.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - the `index` command exposes `--embed-graph-edges/--no-embed-graph-edges` and applies the override to the loaded config before computing the expected index profile.
  - `_legacy_unsuffixed_edge_profile_is_compatible(...)` and related profile normalization helpers preserve read compatibility for caches created before the new profile suffix existed, but only when the effective mismatch is the absent edge-embedding option.
- `src/gloggur/indexer/indexer.py`
  - `_build_graph_edges(..., include_text=self.config.embed_graph_edges)` always extracts structural edges, but only includes edge text when edge embeddings are enabled.
  - `_apply_edge_embeddings(...)` hard-exits unless both `config.embed_graph_edges` and an embedding provider are present.
  - verbose-line metrics track `symbol_chunks` and `graph_edges` separately, and index payload stats expose separate counts for graph-edge vectors.
- `src/gloggur/graph/extractor.py`
  - continues to define deterministic edge text shapes (`EDGE_TYPE:...`) when `include_text=True`; the issue changed when that text is retained/embedded, not the structural graph extraction itself.
- Tests/docs
  - `tests/unit/test_indexer.py`, `tests/integration/test_cli.py`, `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py`, and `tests/unit/test_cli_main_index_additional.py` lock the default-off, opt-in, CLI override, and legacy-profile-compatibility behavior.
  - `README.md`, `docs/QUICKSTART.md`, and `docs/semantic-search-embedding-breakdown-base.md` document the new default and the opt-in path.

Recommended scope interpretation:
- this issue is about default operational cost control and explicit profile semantics for edge vectors;
- it is not a removal of graph edges from the index, and it is not a change to structural graph extraction fidelity.
acceptance_criteria:
- Default config and default `index` runs keep graph edges structural-only: graph edges are still extracted/persisted, but edge text and edge embedding vectors are omitted unless explicitly enabled.
- Operators can enable or disable edge embeddings explicitly through config, environment, and CLI flag overrides.
- The index profile string, status/resume payloads, and cache compatibility logic account for the `embed_graph_edges` flag so edge-vector behavior is not silent state.
- Legacy cache profiles that predate the `embed_graph_edges` suffix remain readable when the missing suffix is the only relevant difference.
- Index stats and verbose metrics separate symbol-chunk vectors from graph-edge vectors so default versus opt-in behavior is externally visible.
- Docs and regression tests reflect the default-off / opt-in contract.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/config.py` currently declares `embed_graph_edges: bool = False` and appends `|embed_graph_edges={int(...)}` to `embedding_profile()`.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` currently exposes `--embed-graph-edges/--no-embed-graph-edges` on `index` and applies the override before computing `expected_profile`.
- `src/gloggur/indexer/indexer.py` shows the exact structural-vs-semantic split:
  - `_build_graph_edges(... include_text=self.config.embed_graph_edges)`
  - `_apply_edge_embeddings(...)` returns immediately unless `config.embed_graph_edges` is true
- `README.md` and `docs/QUICKSTART.md` now document that graph edges are always extracted/stored structurally by default and only receive semantic vectors when operators opt in.
- Focused regression coverage on 2026-03-26:
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_indexer.py::test_indexer_persists_graph_edges_without_edge_embeddings_by_default tests/unit/test_indexer.py::test_indexer_opt_in_restores_edge_text_and_embeddings tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_index_disables_graph_edge_embeddings_by_default_and_opt_in_restores_them tests/unit/test_cli_main_index_additional.py::test_index_cli_overrides_embed_graph_edges_flag tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_profile_reindex_reason_allows_legacy_unsuffixed_edge_profiles_for_reads`
  - result: `6 passed, 3 warnings in 4.72s`
- Unit/integration test behavior currently proves:
  - default indexing persists edges with `edge.text is None` and `edge.embedding_vector is None`
  - opt-in indexing restores `EDGE_TYPE:...` text payloads and non-null edge vectors
  - CLI flag overrides can force the config true or false
  - legacy profiles missing the new `embed_graph_edges` suffix remain acceptable on read paths when that is the only delta
- Direct CLI probe on 2026-03-26 over a tiny Python fixture repo confirmed:
  - default run: `embedded_symbol_vectors=2`, `embedded_edge_vectors=0`, `embedded_vector_count=2`, `graph_edge_count=10`
  - opt-in run: `embedded_symbol_vectors=2`, `embedded_edge_vectors=10`, `embedded_vector_count=12`, `graph_edge_count=10`
  - this confirms the same structural graph survives both modes while only semantic edge vectors change
- History/provenance clue:
  - commit `15dadd7` (`feat(index): disable graph edge embeddings by default + reformatting`) is the landing commit touching config, CLI, indexer, docs, and the regression tests cited above
- Gap between the original low-signal wording and the shipped outcome:
  - the change is not “turn graph edges off”
  - it is “make edge vectors opt-in while preserving structural graph extraction and surfacing the mode in profile/stat contracts”

title: Exclude structural virtualenv roots from indexing
description: gloggur indexing currently relies on hard-coded directory names like venv/.venv and incorrectly indexes Python virtualenv contents when the env directory has an arbitrary name such as _venv. Replace name-based env exclusion with structural detection (pyvenv.cfg, POSIX bin+site-packages, Windows Scripts+Lib/site-packages) and apply it consistently across indexer, symbol indexer, and CLI directory walks. Add regression tests for arbitrary env names and Windows/POSIX layouts.

title: Broaden JS symbol extraction for CommonJS and prototype patterns
description:
Improve `gloggur` JS/TS symbol extraction so CommonJS- and prototype-heavy codebases like Express no longer severely undercount symbols.

## Problem
- Express benchmark is reporting roughly `141 files` and `123 symbols`.
- File count looks plausible; symbol count is likely far too low.
- The extractor appears to miss common real-world patterns such as:
  - `exports.foo = function () {}`
  - `module.exports.foo = function () {}`
  - `proto.method = function () {}`
  - `Constructor.prototype.method = function () {}`
  - object property function assignments
  - accessor definitions where feasible

## Goal
Improve symbol discovery coverage for JavaScript first, and TypeScript where the same AST shapes apply, so Express-like repositories move from obvious undercounting to materially more realistic symbol coverage.

## Scope
This issue covers symbol discovery only.

Out of scope:
- full call graph construction
- type inference
- semantic resolution beyond local syntactic patterns
- runtime export analysis
- dynamic/computed property names in the first version
- alias tracking across multiple hops
- helper-based runtime mutation inference

## Desired Outcome
Continue extracting existing obvious symbols while adding support for:
- named function declarations
- class declarations
- class methods
- methods assigned through prototype/property patterns
- exported function-valued properties
- object literal function-valued properties where symbol naming is recoverable

The result should materially raise symbol counts for CommonJS-heavy repositories like Express.

## Functional Requirements
### 1. Preserve current obvious symbol support
Must continue to extract at least:
- `function foo() {}`
- `class Foo {}`
- class methods
- `const foo = () => {}`
- `const foo = function () {}`

### 2. Add CommonJS export assignment support
Extract symbols from patterns like:
- `exports.send = function send() {}`
- `exports.json = function () {}`
- `module.exports.send = function send() {}`
- `module.exports.json = function () {}`

Preferred canonical names:
- `exports.send`
- `exports.json`
- `module.exports.send`
- `module.exports.json`

Recommended metadata:
- `symbol_name`: canonical identifier
- `display_name`: friendly label
- `container`: `exports` or `module.exports`

### 3. Add prototype/property method assignment support
Extract symbols from patterns like:
- `proto.handle = function handle(req, res) {}`
- `proto.use = function (fn) {}`
- `Router.prototype.route = function route(path) {}`
- `app.listen = function listen() {}`

Preferred canonical names:
- `proto.handle`
- `proto.use`
- `Router.prototype.route`
- `app.listen`

### 4. Add object literal function-valued property support
When assigned to a named binding, extract symbols from patterns like:
- `const api = { render: function render() {}, send() {}, json: () => {} }`

Preferred canonical names:
- `api.render`
- `api.send`
- `api.json`

### 5. Preserve metadata
Every extracted symbol should continue to include:
- file path
- start line / column
- end line / column when available
- symbol kind
- short snippet or AST-backed preview

## Symbol Kinds
Use or add kinds that distinguish these cases where practical:
- `function`
- `class`
- `method`
- `exported_function`
- `prototype_method`
- `object_method`
- `property_function`

Exact taxonomy does not need to be perfect on day one, but `prototype_method` and `exported_function` would be especially useful.

## Naming Rules
Prefer preserving the syntactic access path as the canonical name when present:
- `exports.send`
- `module.exports.json`
- `proto.handle`
- `Router.prototype.route`
- `api.render`

Optionally expose a simplified display name for search/ranking:
- `send`
- `json`
- `handle`
- `route`
- `render`

## Detection Rules
### Rule A: Assignment expression with function-like RHS
Match assignments where:
- lhs is an identifier or member expression
- rhs is a function expression, arrow function, or class expression

Examples:
- `foo = function () {}`
- `obj.bar = function () {}`
- `exports.send = function () {}`
- `proto.use = (fn) => {}`

### Rule B: Symbol-bearing member-expression LHS
Treat these lhs shapes as candidates:
- `Identifier`
- `Identifier.Member`
- `Identifier.prototype.Member`
- `exports.Member`
- `module.exports.Member`

This is syntactic extraction only; do not require full semantic resolution.

### Rule C: Object literal properties under named bindings
When seeing `const obj = { a() {}, b: function () {}, c: () => {} }`, emit:
- `obj.a`
- `obj.b`
- `obj.c`

Only do this when the object is assigned to a named variable / const / let / export target.

## Heuristics
- Favor recall over perfect semantic certainty.
- Do not emit incidental anonymous callbacks such as `array.map(function (x) {})` or `app.use((req, res, next) => {})` unless they are directly assigned to a named symbol-bearing target.
- Avoid duplicate emission. For `exports.send = function send() {}`, emit one symbol record with canonical name `exports.send` and optional intrinsic name `send`.

## Implementation Sketch
Parser traversal should inspect at least:
- `FunctionDeclaration`
- `ClassDeclaration`
- `VariableDeclarator` with function-like initializer
- `AssignmentExpression` with function-like rhs
- `ObjectExpression` assigned to a named binding

Useful helpers:
- `extract_symbol_target(lhs) -> SymbolTarget | None`
- `is_function_like(node)` for function expressions, arrow functions, method-like forms, and class expressions

## Acceptance Criteria
Minimum acceptance tests:
1. CommonJS exports
   - input: `exports.send = function () {}; module.exports.json = function json() {}`
   - expected: `exports.send`, `module.exports.json`
2. Prototype assignments
   - input: `proto.use = function (fn) {}; Router.prototype.route = function route(path) {}`
   - expected: `proto.use`, `Router.prototype.route`
3. Named variable assignments
   - input: `const listen = function () {}; const render = () => {}`
   - expected: `listen`, `render`
4. Object literal methods
   - input: `const api = { send() {}, json: function () {}, end: () => {} }`
   - expected: `api.send`, `api.json`, `api.end`
5. Incidental callbacks do not count
   - input: `items.map(function (x) { return x })`
   - expected: none

## Benchmark Validation
Use Express.js as a regression benchmark.

Before:
- files: about `141`
- symbols: about `123`

After validating this issue:
- file count should stay stable
- symbol count should rise substantially
- extracted symbols should visibly include names from:
  - `lib/application.js`
  - `lib/request.js`
  - `lib/response.js`
  - router-related files
- spot checks should confirm patterns like:
  - `app.listen`
  - `app.use`
  - response/request helpers
  - router prototype methods
  - exported helpers

Exact target count is not required, but the result should no longer look obviously implausible.

## Rationale
Express is a strong stress test because it is small enough to inspect, widely used, and heavy on older but still very common JS/CommonJS patterns. If `gloggur` badly undercounts Express, it is likely undercounting a broad class of real-world JS projects.

title: Make support collect betatester-friendly with repo init and live trace capture
description: Implement a new repo-level gloggur init command with a --betatester-support flag, keep gloggur watch init watch-specific, add automatic support runtime tracing for ordinary commands in initialized repos, capture live index stage/heartbeat and stack dumps for in-flight hangs, make support collect bundle active and recent traces plus smart cache/index artifacts, and update docs and tests so betatesters only need gloggur support collect after odd behavior.

title: Fix find ergonomics follow-up verification regressions
description: The grep-like find ergonomics change added a new CLI error code and several long lines. Update the error-catalog contract fixture to include the new find code and fix Ruff line-length violations in the touched files so the full pytest and static quality gates pass again.

title: Rework gloggur find into a grep-like locator hybrid
description: Make gloggur find accept shell-level grep-like query/path forms, replace forced --about fusion with bounded semantic assist, bias toward locator-style convergence, and emit stronger suggested next commands while preserving stateless behavior and the slim find_v1 contract.

title: Fix regressions from find --about rollout
description: Full-suite verification after adding gloggur find --about surfaced two regressions: docs/error-catalog contract coverage needs the new find_about_contract_conflict code reflected in the fixture expectations, and static quality gates fail on the changed files. Fix both so the feature lands with the repository verification surface green.

title: Revert router locality rollout
description: Rollback the router locality-mode work introduced by commit 3d53002 without reverting the unrelated docs/CLI/help/quickstart changes that were included in the same commit. Restore the targeted router/support/test files to 6324d97, remove src/gloggur/search/router/locality.py, preserve Beads history, and verify with focused plus full test runs.

title: Fix static quality gate failures after router locality work
description: The full pytest run now fails at tests/integration/test_run_static_quality_gates_harness.py because scripts/run_static_quality_gates.py returns a Ruff failure on the verification surface after the router locality changes. Reproduce the gate runner, fix the actual lint violation(s) in the gated files, and re-run the harness plus the full static gate.

title: Collapse router follow-up searches onto local evidence
description: Implement router locality mode after decisive hits: persist a short-lived repo-local anchor, improve first-hit ranking for source behavior spans, bridge to nearby tests, suppress broad confirmation queries after local evidence is found, expose additive ContextPack locality/debug fields, and cover q1/q4/q5-style regressions with unit and CLI tests.

title: Harden public docs/help against promise gaps
description: Fix public-surface contradictions where CLI help and docs over-promise or misdescribe current behavior. Scope: hide removed search/find grounding flags from help while keeping fail-closed compatibility, correct graph neighbors default-direction docs, document current parser known gaps/inspect default scope/security warning behavior, and clarify that quickstart smoke/CI primarily use the deterministic test embedding provider unless a dedicated local-provider verification path exists.

title: Relax default tool-version drift compatibility for reusable indexes
description: Default cache compatibility is too strict across gloggur package version bumps. Pure tool-version drift currently forces reindex_required via tool_version_changed even when schema, embedding profile, and index metadata are otherwise reusable. Change default status/search compatibility policy so tool-version drift is advisory by default, keep explicit incompatibilities as hard blockers, preserve machine-readable provenance, and add regression coverage for reuse across adjacent package versions. discovered-from:bd-oym
close_reason: Closed after fixing static quality-gates follow-up formatting issues

title: Allow tool_version_changed to self-heal after successful reindex
description: A successful incremental index run currently cannot clear marker-only tool-version drift. When the only incompatibility is last_success_tool_version != current tool version, status/search stay reindex_required even after a no-op index run because _persist_last_success_resume_state bails out before advancing last-success markers. Fix the post-index persistence path so a successful index can repair tool-version drift without cache deletion, while keeping status/search fail-closed for real incompatibilities. Acceptance: a successful no-op reindex with unchanged file content updates last_success_tool_version and any stale last-success fingerprint markers as needed, and follow-up status --json returns resume_ok. Add unit and integration regressions for pure tool-version drift repair and preserve unchanged-run idempotence.

title: Fix watch daemon readiness race in CI
description: GitHub Actions CI run 22849457212 failed on required Python 3.12 because the watch daemon can report running before the watcher backend is actually ready. Implement explicit watch readiness state, block daemon start until ready, and harden the watch lifecycle integration test to wait for readiness and use the same config for search.

title: Include byte offsets in find output
description:
Current code already ships additive byte offsets on the agent-first `find` surface. `gloggur find --json` and `gloggur find --stream` now expose `start_byte` and `end_byte` on each emitted hit when the routed search result includes byte spans, allowing agents to round-trip directly into `gloggur extract` without requesting the bulkier `search --json` contract or extra flags.

This change is narrower than “search payload redesign”. `find` still emits the slim `find_v1` contract with `schema_version = 1`, a `decision` block, and a truncated hit list optimized for locator workflows. The byte offsets are an additive field on the per-hit payload, not a contract-family migration. Plain-text `find` output remains the compact rg-like summary and does not print byte offsets.

The current implementation is also broader than a pure CLI formatting tweak. Byte offsets originate upstream in routed hits from the exact, semantic, and symbol backends, are preserved by `ContextHit`, and are then forwarded by the `find` serializers. The practical user-facing effect is that `find` can now serve as the lightweight discovery step in a `find -> extract` workflow without forcing agents to fall back to `search` just to recover byte ranges.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py`
  - exact, semantic, and symbol backends already attach `start_byte` / `end_byte` to backend hits when byte-span information is available.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/engine.py`
  - `_pack_hits(...)` preserves those byte offsets when projecting backend hits into `ContextHit` objects.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/types.py`
  - `ContextHit` models `start_byte` and `end_byte` as additive optional fields and includes them in `to_dict()` when present.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `_build_find_hit_payload(...)` is the narrow feature gate for this issue: it copies `start_byte` / `end_byte` into the slim `find` hit payload when they are integers.
  - `_build_find_success_payload(...)` uses that helper for `find --json`.
  - `_emit_find_success(...)` reuses the same hit payload objects for NDJSON stream mode and keeps plain-text output rg-like by rendering only rank/path/line/score/tags/snippet.
- `src/gloggur/search/compat.py`
  - the legacy search compatibility adapter already maps byte offsets from `hits[]` into compatibility `results[]`, so this issue should be understood as `find` parity with the routed byte-span model rather than a new byte-span primitive.
- docs
  - `README.md` and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` now describe byte-offset availability and the `search/find -> extract` handoff.

Recommended scope interpretation:
- treat this issue as a shipped `find` contract refinement on top of `bd-061`, not as a new extraction subsystem;
- keep future payload growth constrained so `find` stays materially slimmer than `search --json` even while carrying the minimum fields needed for agent round-trips.
acceptance_criteria:
- `gloggur find --json` includes additive `start_byte` and `end_byte` fields on hits by default when routed hits carry byte spans.
- `gloggur find --stream` emits the same per-hit byte offsets in NDJSON mode.
- Plain-text `gloggur find` output remains unchanged in shape and does not expose byte offsets.
- `gloggur search --json` remains on the ContextPack v2 contract and still carries byte offsets; this follow-up does not collapse `search` and `find` into one payload family.
- A hit path plus `start_byte` / `end_byte` can be fed directly into `gloggur extract ... --json` to recover the exact UTF-8 slice represented by the hit.
- Docs and CLI tests describe the inclusive/exclusive byte contract (`start_byte` inclusive, `end_byte` exclusive) and the agent round-trip workflow.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_build_find_hit_payload(...)` conditionally includes `start_byte` and `end_byte` on the slim `find` hit payload.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_emit_find_success(...)` streams those same hit payload objects in NDJSON mode and keeps plain-text output to rank/path/line/score/tags/snippet without printing byte offsets.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/engine.py:_pack_hits(...)` copies `start_byte=hit.start_byte` and `end_byte=hit.end_byte` into `ContextHit`, confirming the CLI is forwarding routed byte spans rather than inventing them.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/types.py:ContextHit` already models byte offsets as first-class optional hit fields.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_find_json_contract_is_slim_and_security_gated` confirms `find --json` stays on `schema_version == 1` / `contract_version == "find_v1"` while emitting hit-level `start_byte == 10` and `end_byte == 18`.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_find_stream_emits_ndjson_hits` confirms `find --stream` emits NDJSON hits with `start_byte` and `end_byte` and no top-level `decision` object per line.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_search_json_contract_remains_contextpack_v2_after_find_addition` confirms `search --json` still uses the separate ContextPack v2 surface while retaining byte offsets.
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_json_emits_contextpack_v2_without_legacy_keys` confirms indexed real CLI output includes `start_byte` / `end_byte` on hits and excludes removed v1 keys.
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_extract_round_trip_for_exact_semantic_and_symbol_modes` proves the practical round-trip: hit `path`, `start_byte`, and `end_byte` feed `gloggur extract ... --json`, and the extracted text matches the raw byte slice.
- Docs touchpoints already landed:
  - `README.md` documents `start_byte` / `end_byte` on search hits and explicitly notes that `find --json` includes byte offsets for exact agent round-trips.
  - `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` documents the `search/find -> extract` workflow and the inclusive/exclusive byte semantics.
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_find_json_contract_is_slim_and_security_gated tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_find_stream_emits_ndjson_hits tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_search_json_contract_remains_contextpack_v2_after_find_addition tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_json_emits_contextpack_v2_without_legacy_keys tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_extract_round_trip_for_exact_semantic_and_symbol_modes`
  - result: `5 passed, 3 warnings in 3.18s`
- Gap between the original short issue text and current shipped behavior:
  - the original wording sounded like a serializer-only field addition;
  - the actual shipped contract spans routed byte-span propagation, slim find hit serialization, NDJSON stream parity, extract interoperability, and explicit preservation of separate `find_v1` vs `search` ContextPack v2 surfaces.

title: Add agent-first find command
description: Implement a new agent-first  surface on top of the existing search router. Preserve  behavior and contracts exactly, factor search execution into a shared helper, add concise text/JSON/stream serializers for , update docs, and add regression coverage proving  is slimmer while  remains unchanged.

title: Artifact provenance and restore trust controls
description: Extend artifact manifests with provenance, add restore-time provenance enforcement and expected-manifest verification, and preserve existing traversal/hash protections.

title: Remote provider and custom endpoint trust controls
description: Gate remote embedding selection and custom base URLs for mixed-trust repos, surface provider/host diagnostics, and keep trusted explicit config/operator intent paths available.

title: Repo-config trust classification and adapter gating
description: Implement config trust classification for explicit vs auto-discovered repo config, expose trust metadata in JSON diagnostics, and stage warnings/default behavior for untrusted repo adapter overrides.

title: Mixed-trust repo hardening
description: Security hardening initiative for Gloggur based on the repository-grounded threat model. Track staged rollout work for repo-config trust classification, adapter/provider gating, artifact provenance, support bundle sensitivity, provider/model availability bounds, and release provenance hardening.

title: Fix regressions from identifier-first search tightening
description: Follow-up regression pass for the identifier-first search tightening and compact agent output changes. Fix the inspect/docstring warning in src/gloggur/cli/main.py, restore structured io_failure behavior when the metadata store cannot open during search --json, and clear static quality gate failures introduced by the change set.

title: Identifier-first search tightening and compact agent output
description: Implement identifier/declaration-first routing for gloggur search, lazy semantic backend initialization, additive compact JSON output with decisive next-action signals, exact-hit ranking improvements, and benchmark-driven regressions for escape_leading_slashes / after_request / url_has_allowed_host_and_scheme style queries. Follow up in gloggur-benchmarking to collapse retry-success command classification so successful gloggur retries are not marked failed overall.

title: Fix docstring and Ruff regressions from language-support rollout
description:
Follow-up bugfix for post-rollout verification failures:
- inspect hotspot docstring test reports Missing docstring symbols in src/gloggur/cli/main.py due to nested helper functions added for skipped-extension diagnostics without docstrings.
- static quality gates fail at Ruff stage due to E501 line-length violations introduced in src/gloggur/cli/main.py and src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py.

Acceptance:
- Add docstrings to flagged nested helpers.
- Reformat long lines to satisfy Ruff in gated files.
- Re-run the two failing integration tests and confirm pass.
notes:
Implemented follow-up regressions fix:
- Added docstrings to nested skipped-extension _record helpers in src/gloggur/cli/main.py so inspect hotspot docstring audit stays clean.
- Wrapped long lines in src/gloggur/cli/main.py and formatted src/gloggur/parsers/support_contract.py to satisfy static quality black/ruff gates.

Verification:
- source .venv/bin/activate && pytest -n 0 'tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_inspect_reports_no_missing_docstrings_for_recent_f11_hotspots[src/gloggur/cli/main.py]' tests/integration/test_run_static_quality_gates_harness.py::test_run_static_quality_gates_harness_succeeds_for_verification_surface -q
- source .venv/bin/activate && python scripts/run_static_quality_gates.py --format json
- source .venv/bin/activate && pytest

title: Add fail-loud unsupported-extension enforcement modes for index/inspect
description: Extend unsupported-extension diagnostics with explicit fail-loud enforcement modes for index and inspect. Add deterministic warning/error contracts, thresholds/policies for mixed-language repos, and docs for operational defaults in agent/CI flows.

title: Improve Go/Rust/Java symbol fidelity and container semantics
description:
Current code already registers `.go`, `.rs`, and `.java` in the default extension map and exposes those languages in the machine-readable support contract, so this is no longer a generic cross-language fidelity bucket. The remaining work is to close specific parser/extraction gaps in `TreeSitterParser` and then bring the published contract and parser-capability corpus up to parity.

Observed gaps as of 2026-03-26:
- Go: `method_declaration` extracts, but named `type X struct {}` and `type X interface {}` declarations are dropped. Receiver methods such as `func (s Service) Run()` are emitted as `kind=method` with `fqname=Run` and `container_fqname=null` instead of `Service.Run`.
- Rust: `struct_item` and `trait_item` extract, but `impl Client { fn parse(...) }` is emitted as `kind=function` with no container, and trait-body signatures such as `trait Repo { fn fetch(&self); }` do not emit method symbols.
